library.name: Basic Writing Frames
library.description: Foundational writing-oriented sentence frames for giving opinions, organizing ideas, adding examples, and making clear academic-style statements.

items.title: I think that...
items.pattern: I think (that) + clause
items.coreExplanation: I think (that) + 从句 用来直接表达“我认为……”。that后面要接完整句子，至少有主语和谓语，如I think (that) he is right. 它通常放在句首先说观点，也可插入句中作较柔和的评论，如He is, I think, ready.
items.usageNote: 语气中性、很常用，适合日常对话、课堂发言、邮件里表达个人看法。比很正式的书面表达更自然，但在学术写作中反复用会显得单一。它说的是“个人判断”，不适合拿来表示明确证据来源。that常可省略，但初学者先保留更稳。放句首最安全；若插入句中，前后通常用逗号隔开。

slotSchema.label: speaker
slotSchema.hint: 通常固定用I think。不要误换成I am think。例：I think ...

slotSchema.label: connector
slotSchema.hint: 可用that，也可省略；后面必须接完整句子。常错：that后只接名词。例：I think that she is busy.

slotSchema.label: opinion clause
slotSchema.hint: 放你认为的内容，要有主语+谓语。常错：只说a good idea。应说it is a good idea。例：he will come soon.

commonErrors: I think him is right.｜错因：think后面不能直接接宾语+谓语残缺结构；这里需要that从句。｜改法：I think (that) he is right.
I think that a good idea.｜错因：that后面必须是完整句子，不是单独名词短语。｜改法：I think that it is a good idea.
I am think that this is useful.｜错因：think表示状态/观点时这里不用be。｜改法：I think that this is useful.
I think will rain.｜错因：从句缺主语。｜改法：I think it will rain.
According to me, this plan is better.｜错因：想表达个人意见时不用according to me，太不自然。｜改法：I think this plan is better. / In my opinion, this plan is better.
I think, he is right.｜错因：句首主句后一般不单独用逗号隔开完整宾语从句。｜改法：I think he is right.

contrasts.title: I think vs. In my opinion
contrasts.note: 结构上，I think后要接完整从句；In my opinion通常作状语放句首/句中，后面直接接整句。语气上，In my opinion更像正式表态，I think更口语自然。对比：I think this is unfair. / In my opinion, this is unfair.

contrasts.title: I think vs. From my point of view
contrasts.note: 结构上，From my point of view是介词短语，不带that从句；I think是主句+从句。语域上，From my point of view更正式、更强调“立场角度”。对比：I think we need more time. / From my point of view, we need more time.

contrasts.title: I think vs. It seems that
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都可接that从句，但I think强调“我的看法”，It seems that强调“根据现象看起来”。语气上，It seems that更客观、更不直接。对比：I think she is tired. / It seems that she is tired.

contrasts.title: I think vs. It appears that
contrasts.note: 结构上都能接完整从句；但It appears that更书面、更正式，常用于报告或较客观判断。I think更适合口语和一般写作。对比：I think the plan failed. / It appears that the plan failed.

contrasts.title: I think vs. according to
contrasts.note: 结构上，according to后接来源，如a report, my teacher，不接个人主观看法；I think直接表达自己的意见。场景上，according to用于引用信息，I think用于表态。对比：According to the report, sales fell. / I think sales will improve.

examples.sentence: I think (that) this book is interesting.
examples.translation: 我觉得这本书很有趣。
examples.note: 最基础、安全的用法；that可省略。

examples.sentence: He is, I think, the best person for the job.
examples.translation: 我认为他是这份工作最合适的人。
examples.note: 句中插入位置，语气稍柔和；前后常用逗号。

examples.sentence: I think that the results show a clear improvement.
examples.translation: 我认为结果显示出明显的提升。
examples.note: 较适合说明性写作或报告。

examples.sentence: I think she is tired, but it seems that she wants to keep working.
examples.translation: 我觉得她累了，但看起来她还想继续工作。
examples.note: 和it seems that对比：前者是主观看法，后者像依据现象的判断。

examples.sentence: I think we should leave now.
examples.translation: 我觉得我们现在该走了。
examples.note: 日常对话中常用来提出建议，语气比直接命令更缓和。

examples.sentence: Do you think that this answer is correct?
examples.translation: 你觉得这个答案对吗？
examples.note: 同一结构也常用于询问对方看法。

templates.label: 安全入门模板
templates.template: I think (that) + [clause].
slotHints: [clause]写完整句子：主语+谓语，如 it is useful / he will come。不要只写名词短语。
templates.exampleSentence: I think that it is a good idea.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我认为这是个好主意。

templates.label: 句中插入模板
templates.template: [sentence part], I think, + [rest].
slotHints: 前后都是同一句内容，I think像插入语；注意前后通常用逗号。
templates.exampleSentence: This plan is, I think, too expensive.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我认为这个计划太贵了。

templates.label: 写作表态模板
templates.template: I think that + [subject] + [verb] + ...
slotHints: 适合短文或邮件先表态；that保留会更清楚。
templates.exampleSentence: I think that our team needs more time.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我认为我们团队需要更多时间。

templates.label: 提建议模板
templates.template: I think + [subject] should + [verb] ...
slotHints: 用should给出较委婉的建议；[subject]可用we/you/he等。
templates.exampleSentence: I think we should check the answer again.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我觉得我们应该再检查一次答案。

items.title: in my opinion / from my point of view
items.pattern: In my opinion, ... / From my point of view, ...
items.coreExplanation: 这个结构用来先明确“下面是我的个人看法”，语气比 I think 更书面、更有组织。后面通常接完整句子，不直接接名词。它最常放在句首并常加逗号：In my opinion, + 句子；From my point of view, + 句子。也可放句中或句尾，但初学者先固定放句首最安全。
items.usageNote: 语气多为中性到较正式，常见于作文、讨论、课堂发言、邮件或需要礼貌表达观点的场景。日常随口聊天更常用 I think。它不适合用来引用事实来源，那要用 according to。放句首时后面通常加逗号；若放句中，要用逗号与主句隔开。初学者建议先用“固定开头+完整句子”，避免后面只接一个名词短语。

slotSchema.label: opinion marker
slotSchema.hint: 固定用 In my opinion 或 From my point of view。常见错误是写成 In my opinion I think... 双重表态。例：In my opinion, this plan is better.

slotSchema.label: full opinion clause
slotSchema.hint: 这里放完整句子，要有主语和谓语；不要只放名词。常见错误：In my opinion, this idea. 应改为 In my opinion, this idea is useful.

slotSchema.label: optional position
slotSchema.hint: 最安全放句首；也可放句中或句尾，但要注意逗号。常见错误是句中不加逗号。例：This plan, in my opinion, will work.

commonErrors: In my opinion, my school policy.｜错在后面只有名词短语，不是完整句子。｜改为：In my opinion, my school policy is too strict.
In my opinion I think this is unfair.｜错在重复表达“我认为”，显得累赘。｜改为：In my opinion, this is unfair. 或 I think this is unfair.
According to me, this movie is boring.｜错在 according to 通常接信息来源，不自然地表示个人意见。｜改为：In my opinion, this movie is boring.
From my point of view this rule is necessary.｜句首后少逗号，书写上不够规范。｜改为：From my point of view, this rule is necessary.
In my opinion, to study abroad.｜错在后面不是完整判断句。｜改为：In my opinion, studying abroad is a good choice.
This plan in my opinion will save money.｜句中插入时缺少逗号，读起来不清楚。｜改为：This plan, in my opinion, will save money.

contrasts.title: vs I think (that)
contrasts.note: 结构上，I think 后面直接接从句；In my opinion 常作插入语放句首并加逗号。语气上，I think 更日常口语；In my opinion 更适合作文和正式讨论。对比：I think it’s too expensive. / In my opinion, it’s too expensive.

contrasts.title: vs according to
contrasts.note: 结构上，according to 后接来源、人或材料，不接“我的判断句”来表示立场；In my opinion 后接完整观点句。语义上，according to 是“根据……”，不是“我认为”。对比：According to the report, sales fell. / In my opinion, the plan failed because of poor timing.

contrasts.title: In my opinion vs From my point of view
contrasts.note: 两者都能引出个人看法，但 From my point of view 更强调“从我的角度看”，稍长、更分析型；In my opinion 更直接、常用。结构上都常放句首加逗号。对比：In my opinion, it’s unfair. / From my point of view, it’s unfair.

contrasts.title: vs It seems that
contrasts.note: 结构上，It seems that 表示“看起来/似乎”，是在根据迹象作判断；In my opinion 是明确表个人立场。语气上，It seems that 更保留、更不强势。对比：It seems that he is upset. / In my opinion, he is upset because of the result.

contrasts.title: vs As for me
contrasts.note: 结构上，As for me 更像转到“至于我”，后面常继续补充态度；In my opinion 直接标记观点。语气上，As for me 较口语、有时偏话题转换；In my opinion 更稳妥。对比：As for me, I prefer tea. / In my opinion, tea is the healthier choice.

examples.sentence: In my opinion, English is easier than math.
examples.translation: 在我看来，英语比数学更容易。
examples.note: 最基础安全用法：句首标记观点，后接完整比较句。

examples.sentence: This restaurant, in my opinion, is too expensive.
examples.translation: 依我看，这家餐厅太贵了。
examples.note: 展示句中位置；前后都要用逗号隔开。

examples.sentence: From my point of view, governments should invest more in public transport.
examples.translation: 从我的角度看，政府应该更多投资公共交通。
examples.note: 较适合写作或正式讨论。

examples.sentence: I think the test was hard, but in my opinion, the listening part was the hardest.
examples.translation: 我觉得这次考试很难，但在我看来，听力部分最难。
examples.note: 用于比较多个看法时，第二句用该结构更清楚地标出立场。

examples.sentence: From my point of view, working from home saves time.
examples.translation: 从我的角度看，居家办公能节省时间。
examples.note: 强调“从某个角度看”的分析感。

examples.sentence: In my opinion, we should leave earlier to avoid traffic.
examples.translation: 我认为我们应该早点出发，避开交通拥堵。
examples.note: 日常生活中也能用，但比 I think 稍正式。

templates.label: Safest beginner template
templates.template: In my opinion, [opinion_clause].
slotHints: [opinion_clause] 放完整句子，如主语+谓语；不要只放名词
templates.exampleSentence: In my opinion, this app is very useful.
templates.exampleTranslation: 在我看来，这个应用很有用。

templates.label: Alternate-position template
templates.template: [main_clause], in my opinion, [rest].
slotHints: [main_clause] 先说主题；插入 in my opinion 时前后都加逗号
[rest] 继续完成主句，不要断成碎片
templates.exampleSentence: This plan, in my opinion, needs more time.
templates.exampleTranslation: 依我看，这个计划需要更多时间。

templates.label: Writing-oriented template
templates.template: From my point of view, [opinion_clause].
slotHints: 适合作文、讨论、演讲；[opinion_clause] 仍然要是完整判断句
templates.exampleSentence: From my point of view, online classes should remain available.
templates.exampleTranslation: 从我的角度看，线上课程应该继续保留。

templates.label: Comparison template
templates.template: In my opinion, [A] is [comparative] than [B].
slotHints: 适合初学者表达比较观点；[comparative] 用 easier/better/more useful 等
templates.exampleSentence: In my opinion, trains are more convenient than buses.
templates.exampleTranslation: 在我看来，火车比公交车更方便。

items.title: it seems that / it appears that
items.pattern: it seems that... / it appears that...
items.coreExplanation: it seems that... / it appears that... 用来把判断说得更谨慎，表示“看起来/似乎……”。后面必须接完整句子，不是单个名词。它常放在句首作引入，也可放在逗号后插入。appears 比 seems 更书面、更客观一些。
items.usageNote: 语气较委婉，适合根据现象做判断、写作中提出谨慎结论、工作学习里的保守表述。seems 更常见于日常和中性表达，appears 常见于书面、报告、说明。不适合你非常确定、已有直接证据、或只是单纯表达个人喜好时。that 后面接完整句子；放句首后常直接继续主句，不要乱断句。初学者先稳用 It seems that + 完整句子。
sceneTags: 描述看起来发生的情况

slotSchema.label: judgment frame
slotSchema.hint: 固定用 It seems that / It appears that。不要说 It seems me that。例：It seems that he's busy.

slotSchema.label: following clause
slotSchema.hint: 这里要放完整陈述句，要有主语和谓语。不要只放名词。例：the train is late / she forgot the key。

slotSchema.label: sentence position
slotSchema.hint: 通常放句首；也可放在较长句前作引入。不要把 that 从句单独切成碎片。例：It appears that the plan will cost more.

commonErrors: Wrong: It seems that his idea. 原因：that 后面不是完整句子。改为：It seems that his idea is practical.
Wrong: It seems me that he is angry. 原因：seem 后不能直接加 me。改为：It seems that he is angry. / It seems to me that he is angry.
Wrong: Seems that he is tired. 原因：正式完整句里通常需要 It。改为：It seems that he is tired.
Wrong: It appears he tired. 原因：后面缺少完整谓语结构。改为：It appears that he is tired.
Wrong: There is no doubt that he may be wrong. 原因：前面绝对肯定，后面却用 may，语气冲突；若想保留判断应改用：It seems that he may be wrong.
Wrong: I like coffee, it seems that. 原因：这个框架不能这样句尾悬空。改为：It seems that I like stronger coffee now. / I like coffee, it seems, because...

contrasts.title: vs I think (that)...
contrasts.note: 结构上，I think 后面也接完整句，但主语是“我”，更像个人观点；it seems/appears 更像根据迹象做判断，语气更客观、更保守。对比：I think he is wrong.（我认为）/ It seems that he is wrong.（看起来）

contrasts.title: vs There is no doubt that...
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都接 that 从句；there is no doubt that 表示非常确定，语气强，常用于有把握的结论。it seems/appears 表示不完全确定。对比：There is no doubt that she won. / It seems that she won.

contrasts.title: vs according to...
contrasts.note: according to 后面接来源，如人、报告、数据；it seems/appears 后面直接接判断内容。according to 强调信息出处，it seems/appears 强调说话人的观察推断。对比：According to the report, sales fell. / It appears that sales fell.

contrasts.title: vs seem to + verb
contrasts.note: 结构上，it seems that + 完整句；主语 + seems to + 动词原形。后者更紧凑，前者更适合整句判断。语气都较委婉。对比：It seems that he knows the answer. / He seems to know the answer.

contrasts.title: vs In my opinion,...
contrasts.note: in my opinion 后面直接接你的看法，主观色彩更明显；it appears/seems 更像从现象出发，不一定强调“我的意见”。写作里若想少一点主观感，可用 it appears that。对比：In my opinion, the rule is unfair. / It appears that the rule is unfair.

examples.sentence: It seems that Tom is busy.
examples.translation: 汤姆似乎很忙。
examples.note: 最安全基础句型：It seems that + 完整句子。

examples.sentence: It seems that we need more time.
examples.translation: 看来我们需要更多时间。
examples.note: 常用于讨论中委婉提出判断。

examples.sentence: It appears that the results were affected by the weather.
examples.translation: 结果似乎受到了天气影响。
examples.note: appears 更像书面或分析性表达。

examples.sentence: I think he is tired, but it seems that he is also upset.
examples.translation: 我觉得他累了，但看起来他也心情不好。
examples.note: 和 I think 对比：后半句更像根据表情现象推断。

examples.sentence: It seems that the bus has already left.
examples.translation: 看来公交车已经开走了。
examples.note: 适合根据眼前情况作出推测。

examples.sentence: It appears that this method is more effective than the old one.
examples.translation: 这种方法似乎比旧方法更有效。
examples.note: 带比较时也保持谨慎，不说得过满。

templates.label: 最稳妥初学者模板
templates.template: It seems that + [clause].
slotHints: [clause] 放完整句子，如 somebody is..., we need..., the bus has left；不要只放名词
templates.exampleSentence: It seems that she is right.
templates.exampleTranslation: 看来她是对的。

templates.label: 书面表达模板
templates.template: It appears that + [clause].
slotHints: [clause] 放较客观的判断内容，适合结果、数据、情况
templates.exampleSentence: It appears that the problem is more serious than we expected.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这个问题似乎比我们预想的更严重。

templates.label: 对比判断模板
templates.template: ..., but it seems that + [clause].
slotHints: 前半句给背景，后半句给根据现象得出的较软判断
templates.exampleSentence: He said he was fine, but it seems that he needs help.
templates.exampleTranslation: 他说他没事，但看起来他需要帮助。

templates.label: 写作引入模板
templates.template: It appears that + [clause], based on + [noun_phrase].
slotHints: [clause] 放结论；[noun_phrase] 放依据，如 the data, the survey, recent evidence
templates.exampleSentence: It appears that sales are falling, based on recent data.
templates.exampleTranslation: 根据最近的数据，销售额似乎在下降。

items.title: according to...
items.pattern: according to + source
items.coreExplanation: according to 用来说明“信息来源”，表示这不是你自己的判断，而是来自某人、某机构、某文件或数据。后面通常接名词短语，如 a report, the news, my teacher，不直接接完整句子。它常放在句首作信息框架，也可放在句末补充来源。
items.usageNote: 语气多为中性到较正式，最自然的场景是报道信息、写作引用、课堂转述、讨论数据。口语里也能用，但如果只是说个人看法，不要用 according to。它后面一般不接逗号内的完整句子来源内容；若放句首，according to + 来源 后常接逗号。初学者最安全的做法是先用“According to + source, + sentence”。

slotSchema.label: 信息来源
slotSchema.hint: 放人、机构、文件、报道、数据等名词短语，如 my teacher, the report, the news。常见错误是放完整句子。例：According to the report, sales rose.

slotSchema.label: 主要信息
slotSchema.hint: 放你要转述的内容，用完整陈述句。常见错误是只写短语不成句。例：According to my doctor, I need more sleep.

slotSchema.label: 句末来源位置
slotSchema.hint: 也可把 according to + 来源 放句末，补充来源。常见错误是位置太靠近所修饰词导致歧义。例：The meeting starts at nine, according to the email.

commonErrors: wrong: According to he, we are late.｜why: according to 后面要接名词宾格或名词短语，不能用主格 he。｜fix: According to him, we are late.
wrong: According to she said, we should wait.｜why: according to 后不能直接接完整从句或“某人说”。｜fix: According to her, we should wait. / She said that we should wait.
wrong: According to, the train is delayed.｜why: according to 后面必须有明确来源，不能省略。｜fix: According to the station staff, the train is delayed.
wrong: I think according to the report sales fell.｜why: 把个人看法和客观来源硬拼在一起，中心不清楚。｜fix: According to the report, sales fell. / I think sales fell.
wrong: According to my opinion, this is wrong.｜why: opinion 不是外部信息来源，这里应表达个人观点。｜fix: In my opinion, this is wrong.
wrong: According to the weather tomorrow will rain.｜why: source 和主句之间缺少清楚边界，且 the weather 不是自然来源表达。｜fix: According to the weather report, it will rain tomorrow.

contrasts.title: vs I think (that)...
contrasts.note: 结构上，according to + 来源 + 句子；I think (that) + 句子。前者强调“消息来自哪里”，后者强调“这是我的看法”。场景上，according to 更适合转述和写作；I think 更适合表达意见。对比：According to the report, prices rose. / I think prices will rise.

contrasts.title: vs in my opinion
contrasts.note: 结构上，according to 后接来源名词；in my opinion 是固定短语，直接表示个人观点。语气上，according to 较客观；in my opinion 主观明显。不要说 according to my opinion。对比：In my opinion, this plan is risky. / According to the report, this plan is risky.

contrasts.title: vs it seems that...
contrasts.note: 结构上，it seems that + 从句；according to + 来源。前者表示“看起来、似乎”，来源不明确；后者明确指出信息出处。语气上，it seems 更不确定。对比：It seems that he is tired. / According to his doctor, he is tired.

contrasts.title: vs the fact is that...
contrasts.note: 结构上，the fact is that + 从句，用来强调说话人认定的事实；according to + 来源 + 从句，只是标明来源。语气上，the fact is that 更强、更像辩论；according to 更克制。对比：The fact is that we are late. / According to the schedule, we are late.

examples.sentence: According to my teacher, this answer is correct.
examples.translation: 根据我老师的说法，这个答案是对的。
examples.note: 最安全基础句型：句首放来源。

examples.sentence: The museum is closed on Monday, according to the website.
examples.translation: 根据网站上的信息，这家博物馆周一闭馆。
examples.note: 句末位置也自然，常用于补充来源。

examples.sentence: According to the report, unemployment fell slightly last year.
examples.translation: 根据这份报告，去年的失业率略有下降。
examples.note: 较常见于说明文、报道、写作。

examples.sentence: According to the map, the station is nearby, but I think we should ask someone.
examples.translation: 根据地图，车站就在附近，但我觉得我们还是该问一下别人。
examples.note: 和 I think 对比：前半句是来源，后半句是个人意见。

examples.sentence: According to the news, a storm is coming tonight.
examples.translation: 根据新闻报道，今晚有暴风雨要来。
examples.note: 日常口语里也很常用。

examples.sentence: According to the instructions, you should press this button first.
examples.translation: 按照说明书，你应该先按这个按钮。
examples.note: 来源也可以是 instructions、rules 等文本。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
templates.template: According to + source, + main_clause.
slotHints: source 放人、机构、报道、文件等名词短语
main_clause 写完整陈述句，不要只写短语
templates.exampleSentence: According to my doctor, I need more exercise.
templates.exampleTranslation: 根据我医生的建议，我需要多运动。

templates.label: 句末补充来源模板
templates.template: + main_clause, according to + source.
slotHints: 先说主要信息，再在句末补充来源
source 仍然用名词短语，不接完整句子
templates.exampleSentence: The train will leave at six, according to the timetable.
templates.exampleTranslation: 根据时刻表，火车六点发车。

templates.label: 写作用模板
templates.template: According to + source, + subject + verb + detail.
slotHints: source 常用 the report, the data, the survey 等
detail 可放数字、变化、结果，使句子更像正式写作
templates.exampleSentence: According to the survey, most students use their phones before bed.
templates.exampleTranslation: 根据这项调查，大多数学生会在睡前使用手机。

items.title: for example / for instance / such as
items.pattern: for example / for instance / such as
items.coreExplanation: 这个组表示“举例说明”，但用法不同：for example和for instance通常引出整个例子，常放句首后加逗号，也可插在句中；such as放在名词后面，后面接一个或几个具体名词，不直接接完整句子。先判断你要补充“整句例子”还是“名词例子”。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，口语和写作都常用，其中for instance稍微更书面一点。适合解释观点、补充说明、写作文、做展示。不适合用来表示顺序（别当成first）或表示递进（别当成besides）。for example/for instance放句首时后面通常加逗号；such as一般直接跟在被说明的名词后，不单独成句。初学者最安全：整句用For example, ...；列举名词用... such as A and B。

slotSchema.label: 整句例子标记
slotSchema.hint: 可用for example或for instance，后面接完整句子。常见错误：后面只接一个孤立名词。例：For example, I study at night.

slotSchema.label: 完整例句
slotSchema.hint: 放一个能独立成句的内容。常见错误：写成不完整片段。例：many students study late ✗；Many students study late. ✓

slotSchema.label: 被举例的名词
slotSchema.hint: such as前面要先有类别名词。常见错误：句子一开始直接Such as books...。例：I like outdoor activities such as hiking.

slotSchema.label: 具体例子
slotSchema.hint: 接一个或多个名词/名词短语。常见错误：such as后接完整句子。例：such as apples and oranges

commonErrors: Such as many fruits are cheap.｜错因：such as后面不直接接完整句子。｜改法：用For example, many fruits are cheap. 或在前面先放名词：fruits such as apples are cheap.
I like such as tennis and swimming.｜错因：such as前面缺少被说明的类别名词。｜改法：I like sports such as tennis and swimming.
For example sports are important.｜错因：句首用for example时通常后面要有逗号，便于看出它是插入性的举例标记。｜改法：For example, sports are important.
I want to improve my skills, such as I practice every day.｜错因：such as不能引出完整分句。｜改法：I want to improve my skills. For example, I practice every day.
First of all, I like cats, for example dogs.｜错因：for example不适合直接挂一个裸名词来列举同类成员；这里应在名词后用such as。｜改法：I like animals such as cats and dogs.
Besides, reading helps us learn. For example means it is first.｜错因：把for example误当顺序标记。｜改法：表示顺序用First/First of all；表示举例用For example, reading helps us learn.

contrasts.title: for example / such as
contrasts.note: 结构上，for example常接完整句子或独立例子；such as接名词短语。语气都中性，但such as更像“列举成员”。对比：For example, I read at night. / I read books such as novels.

contrasts.title: for example / first of all
contrasts.note: 结构上，两者都可放句首，但for example表示“举例”，first of all表示“首先”。场景上，一个是说明，一个是排序。对比：For example, plants need water. / First of all, we need water.

contrasts.title: for example / besides
contrasts.note: 结构上都能放句首加逗号，但for example引出例子，besides添加新信息。语气上，besides有递进补充感。对比：Many fruits are healthy. For example, apples are rich in fiber. / Besides, they are cheap.

contrasts.title: for instance / for example
contrasts.note: 结构几乎一样，都可放句首或句中接例子；语气上for instance稍微更书面一点。多数场景可互换。对比：For example, this plan works. / For instance, this plan works.

contrasts.title: such as / like
contrasts.note: 结构上都可接名词，但such as更明确表示“例如、列举”，写作里更正式；like在口语里常见，有时也像“像”。对比：subjects such as math and history / subjects like math and history

examples.sentence: For example, I study English for thirty minutes every night.
examples.translation: 例如，我每晚学三十分钟英语。
examples.note: 最安全的基础用法：句首举整句例子，后加逗号。

examples.sentence: Many students, for instance, review new words on the bus.
examples.translation: 比如，很多学生会在公交车上复习生词。
examples.note: 句中插入也可以，前后常用逗号隔开。

examples.sentence: Some healthy snacks, such as nuts and yogurt, are easy to prepare.
examples.translation: 一些健康零食，比如坚果和酸奶，很容易准备。
examples.note: such as放在名词后，列举具体成员。

examples.sentence: There are many ways to reduce stress. For instance, taking a short walk can help.
examples.translation: 有很多减压的方法。比如，短暂散步就有帮助。
examples.note: for instance比for example稍微更书面，适合写作。

examples.sentence: Good apps such as dictionaries and flashcard tools can support your learning.
examples.translation: 像词典和抽认卡工具这样的好应用可以帮助你的学习。
examples.note: 这一句能看出such as只能接名词短语，不能换成完整从句。

examples.sentence: We should save money. For example, we can cook at home more often.
examples.translation: 我们应该省钱。比如，我们可以更常在家做饭。
examples.note: 先说观点，再用for example补具体做法。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
templates.template: For example, [example_clause].
slotHints: [example_clause]放完整句子，如I read before bed.
templates.exampleSentence: For example, I read English news in the morning.
templates.exampleTranslation: 例如，我早上读英语新闻。

templates.label: 句中插入模板
templates.template: [main_clause], for instance, [example_detail].
slotHints: 前后都是句子成分；for instance作插入语，前后常加逗号。
templates.exampleSentence: Many learners, for instance, practice speaking with friends online.
templates.exampleTranslation: 比如，很多学习者会和网友练口语。

templates.label: 名词后列举模板
templates.template: [head_noun] such as [example_items]
slotHints: [head_noun]先给类别名词；[example_items]放一个或多个名词，如books and magazines.
templates.exampleSentence: Free activities such as walking and reading can reduce stress.
templates.exampleTranslation: 像散步和阅读这样的免费活动可以减压。

templates.label: 写作展开模板
templates.template: [general_statement]. For instance, [supporting_example].
slotHints: 第一句写总观点；第二句用具体例子支持。
templates.exampleSentence: Online learning is flexible. For instance, students can study at their own pace.
templates.exampleTranslation: 线上学习很灵活。比如，学生可以按自己的节奏学习。

items.title: for one thing... for another...
items.pattern: for one thing..., for another...
items.coreExplanation: for one thing..., for another... 用来把两个不同理由或考虑点清楚地并列出来，意思接近“一个原因是……，另一个原因是……”。两个部分后面通常都接完整句，常放在句首作插入提示，也可放在句中逗号后，帮助读者跟上你的论证。
items.usageNote: 这个结构多见于中性到稍正式的口语和写作，特别适合解释选择、提出反对意见、写短文论点。它自然用于“列出两个理由”，不适合只说一个理由，也不适合强烈对立的两面比较。通常两个提示语后都加逗号；放句中时前后也要注意逗号。初学者最安全的做法是：for one thing, 完整句子; for another, 完整句子。

slotSchema.label: First reason
slotSchema.hint: 这里放第一个完整理由句，最好有主语+谓语。常见错误：只放一个名词短语。例：the job pays well→更自然：it pays well

slotSchema.label: Second reason
slotSchema.hint: 这里放第二个完整理由句，和前面应是不同的理由。常见错误：重复同一意思。例：it is close to home

slotSchema.label: Main claim or decision
slotSchema.hint: 可在前面或后面加总观点，如I don’t want to move. 常见错误：只列理由却不知道在支持什么。例：I chose this school

commonErrors: Wrong: For one thing, because it is cheap, for another, because it is convenient.——错因：because从句单独放这里不完整，两个槽位更适合完整句。改：For one thing, it is cheap; for another, it is convenient.
Wrong: For one thing... for another thing...——错因：固定搭配不是for another thing。改：for one thing..., for another...
Wrong: For one thing, I like it.——错因：只给了一个理由，读者会等待第二点。改：补上for another, ...；或改用first/also等别的连接方式。
Wrong: On the one hand, it is cheap; for another, it is reliable.——错因：混用了两套结构。改：On the one hand..., on the other hand... 或 For one thing..., for another...
Wrong: For one thing I was tired for another I had no time.——错因：缺少逗号，层次不清。改：For one thing, I was tired; for another, I had no time.
Wrong: For one thing, it is cheap; for another, it is very cheap.——错因：第二点只是重复，不是新理由。改：For one thing, it is cheap; for another, it is durable.

contrasts.title: first of all / to begin with
contrasts.note: 结构上，first of all / to begin with 常用于开始列点，后面还能接second, third；for one thing / for another 专门强调“两条理由”。语气上前者更像普通顺序，后者更像论证理由。对比：First of all, it’s cheap. Second, it’s durable. / For one thing, it’s cheap; for another, it’s durable.

contrasts.title: on the one hand / on the other hand
contrasts.note: 结构上，两者都成对出现，但 on the one hand / on the other hand 常呈现两面、利弊或对立看法；for one thing / for another 常是同方向补充理由。语气上前者对比更强。对比：On the one hand, it’s cheap; on the other hand, it breaks easily. / For one thing, it’s cheap; for another, it’s easy to use.

contrasts.title: because
contrasts.note: 结构上 because 直接引出原因从句，通常只连接一个原因；for one thing / for another 用来有条理地列出两个原因。场景上，because 更口语直接；本结构更适合解释和写作。对比：I stayed home because I was sick. / I stayed home, for one thing, I was sick, and for another, the weather was terrible.

contrasts.title: also / besides
contrasts.note: 结构上 also / besides 只是“再加一点”，不要求成对；for one thing / for another 是明显的双理由框架。语气上 also 更随意，论证组织感较弱。对比：It’s cheap and also reliable. / For one thing, it’s cheap; for another, it’s reliable.

contrasts.title: sentence-initial vs mid-sentence
contrasts.note: 结构上本表达最常见在句首：For one thing, ...; for another, ...。也可插入主句后，用逗号隔开。场景上句首更清楚，适合初学者；句中更像自然说明。对比：For one thing, I was busy. / I couldn’t go, for one thing, I was busy.

examples.sentence: I don’t want to buy this car. For one thing, it’s too expensive; for another, it uses too much gas.
examples.translation: 我不想买这辆车。一个原因是它太贵，另一个原因是它太费油。
examples.note: 最安全的核心用法：先说结论，再列两个完整理由。

examples.sentence: For one thing, the apartment is close to my office; for another, the rent is reasonable.
examples.translation: 一个原因是这套公寓离我办公室近，另一个原因是房租合理。
examples.note: 句首直接列理由，常用于回答“为什么选它”。

examples.sentence: The plan should be revised: for one thing, the budget is unrealistic; for another, the timeline is too short.
examples.translation: 这个方案应该修改：一是预算不现实，二是时间安排太短。
examples.note: 较书面，冒号后列出两点理由很自然。

examples.sentence: On the one hand, the job pays well; on the other hand, it is stressful. But I’m still considering it, for one thing, I need the money, and for another, it offers good training.
examples.translation: 一方面，这份工作薪水高；另一方面，它压力大。但我仍在考虑它，一个原因是我需要钱，另一个原因是它能提供很好的培训。
examples.note: 同句展示与on the one hand/on the other hand的区别：前者讲两面，后者讲支持同一决定的两条理由。

examples.sentence: I skipped the party. For one thing, I had an exam the next day; for another, I wasn’t feeling well.
examples.translation: 我没去聚会。一个原因是我第二天有考试，另一个原因是我身体不太舒服。
examples.note: 日常口语中解释决定很自然。

examples.sentence: We chose this school because, for one thing, its teachers are excellent, and, for another, it has a strong music program.
examples.translation: 我们选择这所学校是因为，一方面它的老师很优秀，另一方面它的音乐项目很强。
examples.note: 句中插入也可以，但逗号位置要清楚。

templates.label: Safest beginner template
templates.template: [claim]. For one thing, [reason1]; for another, [reason2].
slotHints: 先写你的结论或决定
第一个完整理由句
第二个完整理由句，和第一个不同
templates.exampleSentence: I won’t join the club. For one thing, I’m too busy; for another, the meetings are late.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我不会加入这个社团。一个原因是我太忙，另一个原因是会议时间太晚。

templates.label: Reason-first template
templates.template: For one thing, [reason1]; for another, [reason2].
slotHints: 完整理由句
另一个完整理由句
templates.exampleSentence: For one thing, the hotel is clean; for another, it’s near the station.
templates.exampleTranslation: 一个原因是这家酒店很干净，另一个原因是它离车站近。

templates.label: Writing-oriented template
templates.template: [claim]: for one thing, [reason1]; for another, [reason2].
slotHints: 适合论文、报告里的主张
第一个书面化理由
第二个书面化理由
templates.exampleSentence: The policy needs revision: for one thing, it is too vague; for another, it is difficult to enforce.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这项政策需要修订：一是它太模糊，二是它很难执行。

templates.label: Mid-sentence template
templates.template: [claim], for one thing, [reason1], and, for another, [reason2].
slotHints: 先写主句
第一个完整理由句
第二个完整理由句
templates.exampleSentence: I chose the train, for one thing, it was cheaper, and, for another, it was more comfortable.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我选择了火车，一个原因是它更便宜，另一个原因是它更舒服。

items.title: first of all / to begin with
items.pattern: first of all, ... / to begin with, ...
items.coreExplanation: first of all 和 to begin with 都用来把“第一点”先说出来，帮助听者或读者跟上你的结构。它们通常放在句首，后面接完整句子，后面常加逗号。最自然的用法是先开头，再继续列出 next, also, finally 等后续内容。
items.usageNote: 语气一般是中性；口语、课堂发言、演讲、说明步骤、邮件和作文里都很常见。它适合用来组织观点或理由，不适合只是随便寒暄时硬加一句。通常放在句首并与主句用逗号隔开；句中偶尔也可用，但初学者先固定用“First of all, + 完整句子”最安全。如果后面没有第二点、第三点，也能用，但最好确实是在强调“先说最重要的第一点”。

slotSchema.label: opening phrase
slotSchema.hint: 可填 first of all 或 to begin with。常见错误是写成 at first（表示“起初”，不是列第一点）。例：First of all, we need a plan.

slotSchema.label: main clause
slotSchema.hint: 后面接完整句子，要有主语和谓语。常见错误是只接名词短语。例：First of all, the room is too small.

slotSchema.label: optional next point
slotSchema.hint: 可继续补充下一点，常和 next, also, finally 连用。常见错误是第一点后完全没有结构呼应却连续堆信息。例：Next, we should check the budget.

commonErrors: Wrong: At first, we need to solve the cost problem. 原因：at first 多指“起初/一开始当时”，不是列举第一点。改法：First of all, we need to solve the cost problem.
Wrong: First of all the price too high. 原因：后面不是完整句子，缺少系动词 is。改法：First of all, the price is too high.
Wrong: To begin with I agree and secondly no. 原因：标点和结构混乱，两个分句直接硬连。改法：To begin with, I agree with the goal. Secondly, I don't like the method.
Wrong: First of all of all, we should wait. 原因：固定搭配重复，结构错误。改法：First of all, we should wait.
Wrong: I first of all think this is useful. 原因：放在动词前很别扭，初学者易误用位置。改法：First of all, I think this is useful.
Wrong: To begin with, the first reason is because it's cheap. 原因：the reason is because 搭配累赘。改法：To begin with, it's cheap. / The first reason is that it's cheap.

contrasts.title: vs. in addition / moreover / besides
contrasts.note: 结构上，first of all/to begin with 负责“开第一点”，而 in addition/moreover/besides 负责“再补充一点”。语气上，moreover 更书面。对比：First of all, it is affordable. In addition, it is easy to use.

contrasts.title: vs. for one thing ... for another ...
contrasts.note: 结构上，for one thing 常要和 for another 连成一组；first of all 可以单独开头。语气上，for one thing 更像口头列理由。对比：For one thing, it's cheap; for another, it's durable. / First of all, it's cheap.

contrasts.title: vs. at first
contrasts.note: 结构上都常放句首，但意思不同。at first 表示时间上的“起初”，first of all 表示顺序上的“首先”。场景上，一个讲过程变化，一个讲组织条理。对比：At first, I was nervous. / First of all, let's check the schedule.

contrasts.title: vs. firstly
contrasts.note: 结构上，两者都能引出第一点；firstly 常和 secondly, thirdly 成套出现。语气上，firstly 略偏书面、列表感更强；first of all 更自然常用。对比：Firstly, we need data. / First of all, we need data.

contrasts.title: vs. to start with
contrasts.note: 结构相近，都可放句首接完整句。语气上，to start with 比 to begin with 更口语一点；to begin with 在口语和写作里都稳妥。对比：To start with, this app is free. / To begin with, this app is free.

examples.sentence: First of all, we need more time.
examples.translation: 首先，我们需要更多时间。
examples.note: 最安全、最通用的基础句型。

examples.sentence: We need more time, first of all, to test the product properly.
examples.translation: 我们首先需要更多时间，来好好测试产品。
examples.note: 句中位置可以用，但不如句首常见；初学者以句首为主。

examples.sentence: To begin with, this report explains the main problem clearly.
examples.translation: 首先，这份报告把主要问题解释得很清楚。
examples.note: 较适合说明文、报告或演讲开头。

examples.sentence: At first, I didn't like the idea. First of all, though, let's look at the facts.
examples.translation: 起初，我不喜欢这个想法。不过首先，我们先看看事实。
examples.note: 用来对比 at first 和 first of all 的不同。

examples.sentence: First of all, turn off the power.
examples.translation: 首先，把电源关掉。
examples.note: 用于步骤说明时很自然，后面通常还有 next/then。

examples.sentence: To begin with, I'd like to thank everyone for coming today.
examples.translation: 首先，我想感谢今天到场的各位。
examples.note: 常见于演讲或正式发言的开头。

templates.label: Safest beginner template
templates.template: First of all, + main_clause.
slotHints: main_clause 填完整句子，如 we need a plan / the room is too small
templates.exampleSentence: First of all, we need a clear plan.
templates.exampleTranslation: 首先，我们需要一个清晰的计划。

templates.label: Alternate opener template
templates.template: To begin with, + main_clause.
slotHints: main_clause 填完整句子，适合演讲、作文、解释理由
templates.exampleSentence: To begin with, this method is easy to use.
templates.exampleTranslation: 首先，这个方法很容易使用。

templates.label: List-building template
templates.template: First of all, + first_point. Next, + second_point. Finally, + last_point.
slotHints: first_point / second_point / last_point 都填完整句子，帮助你清楚列点
templates.exampleSentence: First of all, we need to set a budget. Next, we should compare prices. Finally, we can make a decision.
templates.exampleTranslation: 首先，我们需要定预算。接着，我们应该比较价格。最后，我们可以作出决定。

templates.label: Writing-oriented template
templates.template: To begin with, + topic_clause. In addition, + support_clause.
slotHints: topic_clause 先写第一点；support_clause 再补充另一点，不要把 in addition 放在第一句
templates.exampleSentence: To begin with, public transport is cheaper. In addition, it is better for the environment.
templates.exampleTranslation: 首先，公共交通更便宜。此外，它对环境更好。

items.title: in addition / moreover / besides
items.pattern: in addition / moreover / besides
items.coreExplanation: 这个模式用来“再补充一点”。常见形式有In addition, / Moreover, / Besides, 后面接完整句子；也可用in addition to + 名词/动名词。它们通常放在句首并后接逗号，也可放句中作插入语，但初学者先稳妥地放句首最自然。
items.usageNote: 语气上，moreover最正式，常见于论文、说明文、正式发言；in addition最中性，口语和写作都安全；besides较口语，还常带“而且/再说”意味。适合列举理由、补充优点、推进论证；不适合拿来举例或表示先后步骤。放句首时通常加逗号；句中插入时前后也要注意标点。初学者先用“In addition, + 完整句子”最不容易错。

slotSchema.label: 连接补充词
slotSchema.hint: 可放In addition, Moreover, Besides。常见错误是把正式写作里随便用Besides。例：In addition, the plan is affordable.

slotSchema.label: 完整句子
slotSchema.hint: 后面通常接主语+谓语的完整句。常见错误是只接一个名词。例：Moreover, it saves time.

slotSchema.label: 被补充的对象
slotSchema.hint: 如果用in addition to，后面接名词或动名词，不接完整句。常见错误：in addition to it is cheap。例：In addition to the price, we considered quality.

commonErrors: Wrong: In addition the product is cheap. 原因：句首作连接副词时通常要加逗号。改法：In addition, the product is cheap.
Wrong: Moreover to save money, we walked. 原因：moreover后通常直接接完整句，不接to不定式作主句内容。改法：Moreover, we saved money by walking.
Wrong: In addition to, the product is useful. 原因：in addition to后面必须接名词、代词或动名词，不能直接停住再接完整句。改法：In addition to being useful, the product is cheap.
Wrong: Besides many students like it. 原因：句首连接词后少逗号，书面上不规范。改法：Besides, many students like it.
Wrong: For example, it is cheap. Moreover, such as easy to use. 原因：such as不是独立连接整个句子的补充词。改法：Moreover, it is easy to use. / It has many advantages, such as easy setup.
Wrong: To begin with, it is cheap. In addition, for example, students can afford it. 原因：把“补充点”和“举例”混成一层连接，句子杂乱。改法：In addition, it is affordable. For example, students can buy it easily.

contrasts.title: in addition vs in addition to
contrasts.note: 结构不同：in addition后常接完整句；in addition to后接名词/动名词。语气上都中性，但后者更像“除……之外还”。对比：In addition, it is fast. / In addition to speed, it is cheap.

contrasts.title: moreover
contrasts.note: 结构上也常放句首+逗号+完整句。语体比in addition更正式，常用于论文、报告，不太像轻松聊天。对比：In addition, it is practical. / Moreover, it is practical.

contrasts.title: besides
contrasts.note: 结构上也能放句首接完整句，但语气更口语，还常带“再说、况且”来加强说服。正式论文里不如moreover稳重。对比：Besides, it’s too expensive. / Moreover, it is too expensive.

contrasts.title: first of all / to begin with
contrasts.note: 结构上它们常用来开始第一点，不是单纯“再加一点”。语篇功能是开头列点；in addition是继续补充。对比：First of all, we need data. / In addition, we need time.

contrasts.title: for example / for instance / such as
contrasts.note: 结构和功能都不同：这些是“举例”，不是“新增论点”。语气上可与补充词连用，但不要互相替代。对比：In addition, it is cheap. / For example, students can afford it.

examples.sentence: In addition, the course is easy to follow.
examples.translation: 此外，这门课程很容易跟上。
examples.note: 最安全的基础用法：句首连接词 + 逗号 + 完整句。

examples.sentence: The course is, in addition, easy to follow.
examples.translation: 这门课程另外也很容易跟上。
examples.note: 句中插入也可以，但比句首少见，初学者不必优先模仿。

examples.sentence: Moreover, the results suggest that early training is effective.
examples.translation: 此外，结果表明早期训练是有效的。
examples.note: 更正式，适合论文或报告。

examples.sentence: Besides, it’s cheaper than the other one.
examples.translation: 而且，它比另一个更便宜。
examples.note: 较口语，带一点“再说”的说服感。

examples.sentence: In addition to the price, we considered safety.
examples.translation: 除了价格之外，我们还考虑了安全性。
examples.note: 这里必须用in addition to + 名词，不是完整句。

examples.sentence: In addition, I can work on weekends.
examples.translation: 另外，我周末也能上班。
examples.note: 日常沟通中补充个人条件，很自然。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
templates.template: In addition, + clause
slotHints: 先说完前一点，再用这个模板补充新信息；clause要是完整句，如it is useful / we need more time。
templates.exampleSentence: In addition, we need more time.
templates.exampleTranslation: 另外，我们还需要更多时间。

templates.label: 句中插入模板
templates.template: Subject + be/aux, in addition, + complement
slotHints: 适合已经有主句骨架时插入补充；注意前后逗号。
templates.exampleSentence: The plan is, in addition, affordable.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这个计划另外也负担得起。

templates.label: 名词补充模板
templates.template: In addition to + noun/doing, + clause
slotHints: to后面放名词、代词或动名词，不放完整句。
templates.exampleSentence: In addition to saving money, we reduced waste.
templates.exampleTranslation: 除了省钱之外，我们还减少了浪费。

templates.label: 正式写作模板
templates.template: Moreover, + clause
slotHints: 用于正式补充论点，语气比In addition更书面。
templates.exampleSentence: Moreover, the policy benefits small businesses.
templates.exampleTranslation: 此外，这项政策有利于小企业。

items.title: finally / in conclusion / to sum up
items.pattern: finally / in conclusion / to sum up
items.coreExplanation: finally、in conclusion、to sum up都可用来引出结尾或总结。它们通常放在句首，后面接完整句子，并常用逗号隔开：In conclusion, + 句子。finally也可表示“最后一步/最后发生”，不一定是全文总结，所以写结论时要注意功能是否真是“收束全文”。
items.usageNote: 语气上，in conclusion最正式，常见于作文、报告、演讲结尾；to sum up稍口语一点，也适合总结观点；finally最常见、最直接，但既可表示“最后一点”，也可表示“终于”，所以在正式写作里若要明确“总结全文”，优先用in conclusion。通常放句首，后面加逗号。初学者最安全写法是：In conclusion, + your main point.

slotSchema.label: 结尾标记语
slotSchema.hint: 可用In conclusion, / To sum up, / Finally,. 常见错误：漏逗号或把finally误当“总结全文”万能词。例：In conclusion, we should act now.

slotSchema.label: 总结句
slotSchema.hint: 这里放完整句子，概括你的最终观点。常见错误：只放名词短语，不成句。例：it is important to protect the environment.

slotSchema.label: 补充结果或建议
slotSchema.hint: 可再接一句结果、建议或呼吁。常见错误：一句里塞太多新信息，反而不像总结。例：We must start with small daily changes.

commonErrors: Wrong: In conclusion we should study harder.｜问题：句首结尾标记后通常要加逗号，书面英语更自然。｜修改：In conclusion, we should study harder.
Wrong: In conclusion, the advantages.｜问题：后面只接名词短语，不是完整句子。｜修改：In conclusion, the advantages are clear.
Wrong: Finally, this essay shows social media has risks.｜问题：finally可能更像“最后一点”，不一定明确表示“全文结论”。｜修改：In conclusion, this essay shows that social media has risks.
Wrong: To sum up I think smoking should be banned in public places.｜问题：缺少逗号，书面感不足。｜修改：To sum up, I think smoking should be banned in public places.
Wrong: In conclusion, firstly, students need sleep.｜问题：结论标记后又接首先，逻辑冲突。｜修改：In conclusion, students need enough sleep to learn well.
Wrong: Finally, I arrived at the station.｜问题：这里finally表示“终于/最后发生”，不是总结，不适合当作文结尾模板记忆。｜修改：若表达总结，用In conclusion, ...；若表达“终于”，保留Finally但别当结论句型。

contrasts.title: vs first of all / to begin with
contrasts.note: 结构上，first of all / to begin with用于开头列出第一点；in conclusion / to sum up用于结尾收束。语篇位置完全不同。比较：First of all, we need a plan. / In conclusion, we need action.

contrasts.title: vs in addition / moreover / besides
contrasts.note: 结构上，这些连接词后也常接完整句，但作用是“继续加信息”；in conclusion是“结束并概括”。前者多在中间展开，后者在最后。比较：Moreover, it saves time. / In conclusion, it is worth trying.

contrasts.title: vs finally as sequence marker
contrasts.note: finally既可作顺序词“最后”，也可作结尾标记；in conclusion只表示总结。写步骤、流程时用finally更自然；写文章总结时in conclusion更明确。比较：Finally, add some salt. / In conclusion, home cooking is healthier.

contrasts.title: vs in conclusion
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都常放句首加逗号接完整句。语气上，in conclusion更正式、更像作文结尾；to sum up稍口语，像把前文压缩成一句。比较：In conclusion, the policy should continue. / To sum up, the policy works well.

contrasts.title: vs all in all
contrasts.note: 结构上都可句首接逗号，但all in all更口语、更像日常总结；in conclusion更适合学校写作或正式发言。比较：All in all, the trip was great. / In conclusion, the project was successful.

examples.sentence: In conclusion, exercise is essential for a healthy life.
examples.translation: 总之，锻炼对健康生活至关重要。
examples.note: 最安全的基础结论句型：结尾标记放句首，后接完整观点句。

examples.sentence: Exercise is essential for a healthy life. To sum up, everyone should make time for it.
examples.translation: 锻炼对健康生活至关重要。总的来说，每个人都应该为此安排时间。
examples.note: 前一句先陈述，后一句再单独总结，适合段落收尾。

examples.sentence: In conclusion, this report suggests that public transport should be improved.
examples.translation: 总之，本报告认为应该改善公共交通。
examples.note: 较正式，适合报告、演讲稿、说明文。

examples.sentence: Finally, online classes are useful, but face-to-face learning is still important.
examples.translation: 最后，网课很有用，但面对面学习仍然重要。
examples.note: 这里finally更像“最后一点”，不是强烈的全文总结，和in conclusion语气不同。

examples.sentence: To sum up, we had some problems, but the event was a success.
examples.translation: 总的来说，我们遇到了一些问题，但活动还是成功的。
examples.note: 口头总结和非正式展示中很自然。

examples.sentence: All in all, it was an expensive trip. In conclusion, however, it was worth it.
examples.translation: 总的来说，这趟旅行很贵。不过最后可以说，它还是值得的。
examples.note: 展示口语总结与正式书面收尾的语气差别。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
templates.template: In conclusion, {main_point}.
slotHints: {main_point}放你的最终观点完整句，如it is important to save time
templates.exampleSentence: In conclusion, it is important to protect wild animals.
templates.exampleTranslation: 总之，保护野生动物很重要。

templates.label: 替代结尾模板
templates.template: To sum up, {main_point}.
slotHints: {main_point}放压缩后的总观点，避免加入太多新细节
templates.exampleSentence: To sum up, regular exercise benefits both body and mind.
templates.exampleTranslation: 总的来说，规律运动对身心都有好处。

templates.label: 写作型双句模板
templates.template: In conclusion, {main_point}. {support}.
slotHints: {main_point}放核心结论句
{support}放建议、结果或呼吁句
templates.exampleSentence: In conclusion, school uniforms are useful. They can reduce pressure on students.
templates.exampleTranslation: 总之，校服是有用的。它们可以减少学生的压力。

templates.label: 列点后的最后一点模板
templates.template: Finally, {main_point}.
slotHints: {main_point}放“最后一点”而非全文总括，常用于列举时的最后一条
templates.exampleSentence: Finally, students need enough sleep to study well.
templates.exampleTranslation: 最后，学生需要充足睡眠才能学好。

items.title: on the one hand... on the other hand...
items.pattern: on the one hand..., on the other hand...
items.coreExplanation: on the one hand..., on the other hand... 用来把同一件事的两个相对面并列说清，帮助表达“有利也有不利/一方面也另一方面”。两部分后面通常都接完整分句，常放在句首并用逗号隔开，也可放在句中作插入对比。
items.usageNote: 语气中性偏书面，最自然于作文、报告、讨论优缺点、做平衡判断时。日常口语也能用，但若只是简单转折，常用 but 更自然。不适合列三个以上要点，也不适合前后不是同一主题的内容。写作时通常两边都保留，后面常加逗号；初学者先稳妥地写成“On the one hand, S+V. On the other hand, S+V.”。

slotSchema.label: first side
slotSchema.hint: 填一个完整分句，说明第一面，如优点、原因或支持点。常错：只放名词短语。应写成完整句。例：it saves time

slotSchema.label: second side
slotSchema.hint: 填一个完整分句，说明相对的另一面，内容要和第一面围绕同一主题。常错：写成无关新话题。例：it can be expensive

slotSchema.label: shared topic (optional)
slotSchema.hint: 两边默认谈同一对象，可在前句先点明主题。常错：两边主语完全换掉导致不平衡。例：As for online learning, ...

commonErrors: On the one hand, the price. On the other hand, it is convenient.——前半边只是名词短语，不是完整分句，结构不平衡。改为：On the one hand, the price is low. On the other hand, it is convenient.
On one hand, ..., on the other hand, ...——固定搭配通常说 on the one hand，不要漏掉 the。改为：On the one hand, ..., on the other hand, ...
On the one hand, it is cheap. But on the other hand, it is slow.——中间再加 but 往往重复，框架本身已表示对比。改为：On the one hand, it is cheap. On the other hand, it is slow.
On the one hand, I like tea. On the other hand, my brother lives in Beijing.——前后不是同一议题，听起来像硬拼。改为围绕同一主题：On the one hand, I like tea. On the other hand, coffee keeps me awake longer.
On the one hand, studying abroad is exciting, on the other hand, difficult.——后半边省略过头，不够完整，初学者容易出错。改为：On the one hand, studying abroad is exciting. On the other hand, it is difficult.
On the one hand, ..., on the other hand, ..., on the third hand, ...——这个框架通常只用于两面，不自然地继续列第三面。改为用 first, second, third 或 another point is ...

contrasts.title: for one thing... for another...
contrasts.note: 结构上两边也成对，但更常列举两个理由，不一定形成正反对照；语气更口语、较轻。on the one hand...on the other hand... 更强调同一问题的两面。对比：For one thing, it’s cheap; for another, it’s easy to use. / On the one hand, it’s cheap; on the other hand, it breaks easily.

contrasts.title: but / however
contrasts.note: 结构上 but/however 只表示单次转折，不要求成对框架；语气更灵活，口语里更常见。on the one hand...on the other hand... 更有“有条理地权衡”感觉。对比：It’s cheap, but it’s unreliable. / On the one hand, it’s cheap; on the other hand, it’s unreliable.

contrasts.title: while / whereas
contrasts.note: 结构上 while/whereas 常直接连接两个分句；whereas 更书面。它们重在并列对比，不一定带“权衡利弊”的语气。on the one hand...on the other hand... 更像主动摆出两面。对比：This plan is cheap, whereas that one is faster. / On the one hand, this plan is cheap; on the other hand, it is slower.

contrasts.title: in my opinion
contrasts.note: 结构上 in my opinion 只是引出个人看法，后面仍需自己组织内容；它不自带两面框架。可与本结构连用，但功能不同。对比：In my opinion, the plan is risky. / In my opinion, on the one hand, it saves money; on the other hand, it takes more time.

contrasts.title: on the contrary
contrasts.note: 结构上 on the contrary 用来反驳前面的否定或错误看法，不是简单列出另一面；语气更强。很多学习者会误拿它代替 on the other hand。对比：It isn’t expensive. On the contrary, it’s quite affordable. / On the one hand, it’s affordable; on the other hand, maintenance costs are high.

examples.sentence: On the one hand, this app is easy to use. On the other hand, it lacks some important features.
examples.translation: 一方面，这个应用很容易使用；另一方面，它缺少一些重要功能。
examples.note: 最稳妥的基础写法：两边都用完整句。

examples.sentence: This job, on the one hand, offers a high salary; on the other hand, it requires long hours.
examples.translation: 这份工作一方面工资高；另一方面，它要求工作时间很长。
examples.note: 也可放在句中作插入语，常用分号帮助分清两部分。

examples.sentence: On the one hand, online learning increases access to education; on the other hand, it may reduce face-to-face interaction.
examples.translation: 一方面，线上学习扩大了受教育机会；另一方面，它可能减少面对面互动。
examples.note: 较适合作文和报告中的书面表达。

examples.sentence: On the one hand, living in the city is convenient. On the other hand, living in the countryside is quieter.
examples.translation: 一方面，住在城市更方便；另一方面，住在乡村更安静。
examples.note: 这是“比较两种选择”的用法，不是同一对象的优缺点，但两边仍需围绕同一话题。

examples.sentence: On the one hand, I want to save money. On the other hand, I really need a new laptop.
examples.translation: 一方面，我想省钱；另一方面，我确实需要一台新笔记本电脑。
examples.note: 很常见的日常“内心两难”表达。

examples.sentence: On the one hand, the policy could boost growth; on the other hand, it might widen inequality.
examples.translation: 一方面，这项政策可能促进增长；另一方面，它也可能扩大不平等。
examples.note: 适合新闻评论、报告、议论文中的平衡判断。

templates.label: Safest beginner template
templates.template: On the one hand, {sideA}. On the other hand, {sideB}.
slotHints: {sideA}=完整分句，写第一面，如 it is convenient
{sideB}=完整分句，写相对另一面，如 it is expensive
templates.exampleSentence: On the one hand, the course is interesting. On the other hand, it is very demanding.
templates.exampleTranslation: 一方面，这门课很有趣；另一方面，它要求很高。

templates.label: Alternate-position template
templates.template: {topic}, on the one hand, {sideA}; on the other hand, {sideB}.
slotHints: {topic}=先点明主题，如 This plan
{sideA}/{sideB}=围绕同一主题的两面，尽量结构平行
templates.exampleSentence: This plan, on the one hand, saves time; on the other hand, it costs more.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这个方案一方面节省时间；另一方面，成本更高。

templates.label: Writing-oriented template
templates.template: On the one hand, {sideA}; on the other hand, {sideB}. Therefore, {result}.
slotHints: 前两槽写两面权衡
{result}=你的总结或结论，如 we should be cautious
templates.exampleSentence: On the one hand, tourism creates jobs; on the other hand, it can damage the environment. Therefore, local governments should manage it carefully.
templates.exampleTranslation: 一方面，旅游业创造就业；另一方面，它也可能破坏环境。因此，地方政府应谨慎管理。

items.title: as a result, ...
items.pattern: as a result, statement
items.coreExplanation: as a result 用来把前面已经说过的原因、过程或情况，清楚地总结成“结果是……”。后面必须接完整句子，不能只接一个名词。它最常放在句首，后面通常加逗号：As a result, + 完整句子。也可放句中连接前后信息。
items.usageNote: 这个表达偏书面、说明性，比 so 更正式，常见于作文、报告、分析、新闻说明等需要明确因果的场合。日常聊天能用，但会稍显正式；随口对话里常更自然地说 so 或 that’s why。不适合拿来举例，也不要放在一句话末尾单独悬空。初学者最安全的做法是：先说原因句，再另起一句写 As a result, + 完整句子。

slotSchema.label: 结果分句
slotSchema.hint: 这里必须放完整句子，要有主语和谓语。常见错误：只放名词或短语。例：As a result, sales increased.

slotSchema.label: 前文原因/背景
slotSchema.hint: 通常前一句先交代原因、变化或背景，再用 as a result 引出结果。常见错误：前文没有可推导的原因。例：It rained all night. As a result, the road flooded.

slotSchema.label: 句中位置
slotSchema.hint: 也可放在句中，前后都要和完整分句搭配。常见错误：直接夹在主谓之间。例：The store closed early and, as a result, many customers left.

commonErrors: As a result of, the match was canceled.｜错因：把 as a result 和 as a result of 混用了；前者后面接完整句子，后者后面接名词短语。｜改法：As a result, the match was canceled. / As a result of the rain, the match was canceled.
As a result the heavy rain.｜错因：后面只接名词短语，不是完整句子。｜改法：As a result, it rained heavily 是错的思路；应说 As a result, the game was canceled. 或 As a result of the heavy rain, the game was canceled.
He was late. So as a result, he missed the train.｜错因：so 和 as a result 都在表达结果，叠加显得重复累赘。｜改法：He was late. As a result, he missed the train. / He was late, so he missed the train.
I forgot my key, as a result I couldn't get in.｜错因：连接成分前后标点不规范，书面语里通常要在 as a result 后加逗号。｜改法：I forgot my key. As a result, I couldn't get in. / I forgot my key, and as a result, I couldn't get in.
As a result, of the delay, we arrived late.｜错因：错误地把 of 拆开插进 as a result 结构。｜改法：As a result of the delay, we arrived late. / There was a delay. As a result, we arrived late.

For example, many workers left the company. As a result means this.｜错因：把 as a result 当成“比如”来解释，功能混淆。｜改法：for example 用于举例；as a result 用于说结果。例：Many workers left the company. As a result, the project slowed down.

contrasts.title: as a result vs therefore
contrasts.note: 结构上，两者后面都接完整句子；therefore 更像正式逻辑连接词，常见于论证，语气更书面。as a result 更强调“由前文情况导致的结果”。对比：The cost rose. As a result, profits fell. / The cost rose; therefore, profits fell.

contrasts.title: as a result vs so
contrasts.note: 结构上，so 常直接放在两个分句之间，口语很常见；as a result 常放句首并带逗号。语体上，so 更口语，as a result 更说明文。对比：It was raining, so we stayed home. / It was raining. As a result, we stayed home.

contrasts.title: as a result vs that's why
contrasts.note: 结构上，that's why 后面常接完整句子或结果说明，主语带有“那就是为什么”；as a result 是纯连接框架。语气上，that's why 更口语、更解释性。对比：I was sick. That's why I missed class. / I was sick. As a result, I missed class.

contrasts.title: as a result vs for example
contrasts.note: 结构上，for example 用来引出例子，不表示因果结果；as a result 专门引出结果。场景上，一个是举例，一个是总结后果。对比：Many cities face pollution. For example, Delhi has severe smog. / Many factories closed. As a result, unemployment rose.

contrasts.title: as a result vs as a result of
contrasts.note: 结构差别最重要：as a result + 完整句子；as a result of + 名词短语。语气都偏书面，但后者更适合把“原因”压缩成短语。对比：The flight was delayed. As a result, we arrived late. / As a result of the delay, we arrived late.

examples.sentence: The road was icy. As a result, several schools closed.
examples.translation: 路面结冰了。结果，几所学校停课了。
examples.note: 最基础用法：前一句给原因，后一句用 as a result 引出结果。

examples.sentence: The company lost a major client and, as a result, had to cut costs.
examples.translation: 公司失去了一个大客户，因此不得不削减成本。
examples.note: 句中位置也可以，但前后仍要连接完整信息。

examples.sentence: The survey response rate was low. As a result, the findings should be interpreted with caution.
examples.translation: 问卷回收率很低。因此，调查结果应谨慎解读。
examples.note: 很适合写作和报告中的正式说明。

examples.sentence: It rained all weekend. As a result, we canceled the picnic.
examples.translation: 整个周末都在下雨。结果，我们取消了野餐。
examples.note: 和 so 相比更书面；在作文里更稳妥。

examples.sentence: Many workers left the factory. As a result, production fell sharply.
examples.translation: 许多工人离开了工厂。结果，产量大幅下降。
examples.note: 突出前因后果，不是举例。

examples.sentence: He didn't back up the file. As a result, he lost all his work.
examples.translation: 他没有备份文件。结果，他丢失了所有工作内容。
examples.note: 日常也能用，但口语里可换成 so。

templates.label: 安全初学者模板
templates.template: [原因句]. As a result, [结果分句].
slotHints: 先写一个清楚的原因句
结果部分必须是完整句子，有主语和谓语
templates.exampleSentence: The bus was late. As a result, I missed the meeting.
templates.exampleTranslation: 公交车晚点了。结果，我错过了会议。

templates.label: 句中连接模板
templates.template: [分句1], and as a result, [分句2].
slotHints: 前半句说明变化或事件
后半句写由此产生的结果
templates.exampleSentence: The store closed early, and as a result, many customers went elsewhere.
templates.exampleTranslation: 商店提前关门了，因此很多顾客去了别处。

templates.label: 写作用正式模板
templates.template: [背景/数据句]. As a result, [结论或影响分句].
slotHints: 前句适合写事实、调查、变化
后句适合写影响、后果、建议
templates.exampleSentence: Energy prices increased significantly. As a result, household costs rose.
templates.exampleTranslation: 能源价格显著上涨。因此，家庭开支增加了。

items.title: the fact is that...
items.pattern: the fact is that + clause
items.coreExplanation: the fact is that + 从句 用来把“真正重要的情况/核心事实”明确说出来，常带有澄清、纠正或收束争论的作用。that 后面必须接完整陈述句，不能只接名词。整个结构通常放在句首单独引出观点，也可放在逗号后作插入说明。
items.usageNote: 语气偏正式到中性，常见于议论、解释、课堂发言、邮件和作文中，适合强调“关键点其实是……”。不太适合非常随意的闲聊中反复使用，否则会显得生硬或像在辩论。后面通常直接接完整句子；放句首时后面常不停顿直接说，也可写成 The fact is, ... 但初学者最稳妥是保留 that。想安全使用时，把它当成“先亮出核心事实，再补充理由”的开头框架。

slotSchema.label: 固定开头
slotSchema.hint: 用 The fact is that。常见错误是漏掉 is 或写成 the fact that is。例：The fact is that we need more time.

slotSchema.label: 完整从句
slotSchema.hint: 这里放完整陈述句，要有主语和谓语。不要只放名词短语。例：the project is too expensive.

slotSchema.label: 后续补充
slotSchema.hint: 可在后面再补原因、对比或结果。常见错误是前面说完就接不完整碎片。例：The fact is that he was ill, so he stayed home.

commonErrors: × The fact is that the high cost. 错因：that 后面不是完整句子，只是名词短语。改为：The fact is that the cost is high.
× The fact that is we are late. 错因：is 放错位置，固定结构应是 the fact is that... 改为：The fact is that we are late.
× The fact is that to finish on time. 错因：that 后面不能只接不定式短语。改为：The fact is that we cannot finish on time.
× The fact is that, we need help. 错因：that 后面一般不立刻加逗号，逗号把从句切断了。改为：The fact is that we need help.
× The fact is he is busy. 错因：口语里可省 that，但初学者容易结构不稳。更安全：The fact is that he is busy.
× The fact is that because it rained. 错因：because 从句不能单独充当完整主句内容。改为：The fact is that it rained heavily, so the game was canceled.

contrasts.title: vs I think (that)...
contrasts.note: 结构上，I think that 后面也是从句，但它强调“我的看法”；the fact is that 强调“关键现实”。语气上前者更主观、更日常，后者更像在澄清或定调。对比：I think that he is wrong. / The fact is that he is wrong.

contrasts.title: vs In my opinion, ...
contrasts.note: 结构上，In my opinion 是介词短语，后面直接接完整句；the fact is that 是完整主句框架。语气上前者明确标记个人意见，后者把内容包装成核心事实，更有论证感。对比：In my opinion, this plan is risky. / The fact is that this plan is risky.

contrasts.title: vs It seems that...
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都接从句；但 it seems that 表示根据现象做推测，不完全确定，the fact is that 表示说话人认为情况很明确。场景上前者适合委婉观察，后者适合直接澄清。对比：It seems that she forgot. / The fact is that she forgot.

contrasts.title: vs There is no doubt that...
contrasts.note: 结构上都可接 that 从句；there is no doubt that 语气更强，表示“毫无疑问”，常见于更正式、更强硬的论证。the fact is that 虽强调现实，但不一定带绝对判断。对比：There is no doubt that he is talented. / The fact is that he is talented.

contrasts.title: vs according to + source
contrasts.note: 结构上 according to 后接信息来源，不直接接完整事实从句；the fact is that 直接引出说话人要强调的内容。场景上 according to 用于引用来源，the fact is that 用于自己总结关键点。对比：According to the report, sales fell. / The fact is that sales fell.

examples.sentence: The fact is that I don't have enough money.
examples.translation: 事实是我没有足够的钱。
examples.note: 最基础、最安全的用法：后面直接接完整句。

examples.sentence: I wanted to go; the fact is that I was too tired.
examples.translation: 我本来想去；但事实是我太累了。
examples.note: 可放在分号后，用来补出真正原因。

examples.sentence: The fact is that many students need more time to develop writing skills.
examples.translation: 事实是，很多学生需要更多时间来培养写作能力。
examples.note: 适合作文和课堂讨论，语气较中性正式。

examples.sentence: It seems that he is careless, but the fact is that he is just inexperienced.
examples.translation: 看起来他很粗心，但事实是他只是缺乏经验。
examples.note: 用于和较弱判断对比，突出“真实情况”。

examples.sentence: The fact is that we can't finish this today.
examples.translation: 事实是我们今天做不完这个。
examples.note: 常用于团队沟通，直接说明限制。

examples.sentence: The fact is that she never received my message.
examples.translation: 事实是她从来没收到我的消息。
examples.note: 适合澄清误会。

templates.label: 最安全基础模板
templates.template: The fact is that + 完整句子.
slotHints: 后面放有主语和谓语的完整陈述句，如 I am busy / we need more time，不要只放名词或 because 短语
templates.exampleSentence: The fact is that we need more time.
templates.exampleTranslation: 事实是我们需要更多时间。

templates.label: 对比澄清模板
templates.template: ... , but the fact is that + 完整句子.
slotHints: 前半句先写别人以为的情况或表面现象，后半句写你要纠正的真实情况
templates.exampleSentence: People think it is easy, but the fact is that it takes years of practice.
templates.exampleTranslation: 人们觉得这很容易，但事实是这需要多年的练习。

templates.label: 插入解释模板
templates.template: ... ; the fact is that + 完整句子.
slotHints: 前面先说结果或表面决定，分号后补充真正原因；书面语里很自然
templates.exampleSentence: I can't join the meeting; the fact is that I have a doctor's appointment.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我不能参加会议；事实是我约了医生。

templates.label: 写作用论点模板
templates.template: The fact is that + 完整句子, and this means + 完整句子.
slotHints: 第一部分写核心事实，第二部分写由此带来的结论，适合作文论述
templates.exampleSentence: The fact is that many children sleep too little, and this means schools should educate parents about healthy routines.
templates.exampleTranslation: 事实是很多孩子睡眠不足，这意味着学校应该教育家长建立健康作息。

items.title: there is no doubt that...
items.pattern: there is no doubt that + clause
items.coreExplanation: there is no doubt that... 用来先搭一个“毫无疑问”的框架，再接完整陈述句，表示说话人非常确定。that 后面通常放主语+谓语的从句，不能只放名词。这个结构常放在句首引出观点，也可放在较长句中作主句，语气比 I think 强得多。
items.usageNote: 这是偏正式、偏书面的肯定表达，常见于议论文、报告、演讲、评论和较郑重的讨论。适合强调“结论很明确”时使用；不适合证据不足、只是猜测，或很随便的聊天中频繁使用，否则会显得过重。that 后面要接完整句子；前面通常单独成主句，后面不用再加重复的主观标记。初学者最安全的用法是句首直接说：There is no doubt that + 完整句子。

slotSchema.label: 固定框架
slotSchema.hint: 固定写 There is no doubt that。常见错误是漏掉 that 或写成 There has no doubt。例：There is no doubt that...

slotSchema.label: 后面的完整句子
slotSchema.hint: 放完整陈述句，要有主语和谓语。不要只放名词或短语。例：the plan will succeed

slotSchema.label: 补充依据（可选）
slotSchema.hint: 可在后面继续补充原因、数据或时间状语。常见错误是把依据塞进 that 前面打断结构。例：..., given the recent data

commonErrors: There is no doubt the plan will work.｜错误在于正式基础用法里 that 不应省掉，少了连接词结构不完整。｜改为：There is no doubt that the plan will work.
There is no doubt about that he is right.｜错误在于把 about 和 that 从句混用了。about 后面接名词/动名词，不接完整从句。｜改为：There is no doubt that he is right. / There is no doubt about his ability.
There is no doubt that his success.｜错误在于 that 后面不是完整句子，只有名词短语。｜改为：There is no doubt that he will succeed. / There is no doubt about his success.
I think there is no doubt maybe he will come.｜错误在于确定表达和 maybe 冲突，语气自相矛盾。｜改为：There is no doubt that he will come. 或 It seems that he may come.
There are no doubt that students need sleep.｜错误在于固定表达应为 There is no doubt，不用复数 are。｜改为：There is no doubt that students need sleep.
There is no doubt that, he made a mistake.｜错误在于 that 后面不直接加逗号，逗号把从句拆开了。｜改为：There is no doubt that he made a mistake.

contrasts.title: vs I think (that)...
contrasts.note: 结构上，I think 后也接从句；但 I think 明确是个人看法，语气较软、日常更常见。There is no doubt that 更像强结论，正式度更高。对比：I think he is right.（我觉得）/ There is no doubt that he is right.（几乎确定）

contrasts.title: vs In my opinion,...
contrasts.note: 结构上，In my opinion 是插入短语，后面直接接完整句；There is no doubt that 是完整主句+that从句。前者强调“这是我的观点”，后者强调“这点很确定”。对比：In my opinion, this policy is useful. / There is no doubt that this policy is useful.

contrasts.title: vs It seems that...
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都可接 that 从句；但 it seems that 表示根据现象作推测，语气不确定。There is no doubt that 表示几乎没有怀疑。对比：It seems that he is tired. / There is no doubt that he is tired.

contrasts.title: vs The fact is that...
contrasts.note: 结构上都接 that 从句；The fact is that 常用来引出真实情况、纠正误解，语气是“事实是……”。There is no doubt that 重点不是揭示事实，而是强调确定性。对比：The fact is that we are late. / There is no doubt that we are late.

contrasts.title: vs according to + source
contrasts.note: 结构上 according to 后接来源，不直接表达说话人确信；There is no doubt that 后接完整判断句。前者像“依据某来源”，后者像“我明确下结论”。对比：According to the report, sales rose. / There is no doubt that sales rose.

examples.sentence: There is no doubt that exercise is good for your health.
examples.translation: 毫无疑问，锻炼对你的健康有好处。
examples.note: 最基础、最安全的句首模板。

examples.sentence: After seeing the results, there is no doubt that our team made the right decision.
examples.translation: 看到结果后，毫无疑问，我们团队做出了正确的决定。
examples.note: 放在时间状语后，属于句中主句位置变化。

examples.sentence: There is no doubt that climate change requires immediate action.
examples.translation: 毫无疑问，气候变化需要立即采取行动。
examples.note: 典型正式写作句。

examples.sentence: It seems that the plan is useful, but there is no doubt that it will cost more.
examples.translation: 这个计划似乎有用，但毫无疑问，它会花费更多。
examples.note: 和 it seems that 对比，突出确定与不确定。

examples.sentence: There is no doubt that she will pass the exam if she keeps studying like this.
examples.translation: 如果她继续这样学习，毫无疑问她会通过考试。
examples.note: 日常也能用，但语气比普通聊天更郑重。

examples.sentence: There is no doubt that the policy has improved access to education.
examples.translation: 毫无疑问，这项政策改善了受教育机会。
examples.note: 适合报告、评论、书面分析。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
templates.template: There is no doubt that + [完整句子].
slotHints: [完整句子]放主语+谓语，如: he is right / the plan will work
templates.exampleSentence: There is no doubt that the plan will work.
templates.exampleTranslation: 毫无疑问，这个计划会奏效。

templates.label: 带前置信息模板
templates.template: After/From/Given + [信息], there is no doubt that + [完整句子].
slotHints: 前面放时间、依据或背景；后面仍然必须是完整句子
templates.exampleSentence: Given these numbers, there is no doubt that sales have increased.
templates.exampleTranslation: 从这些数字来看，毫无疑问，销售额已经增长了。

templates.label: 写作用对比模板
templates.template: It seems that + [较弱判断], but there is no doubt that + [较强结论].
slotHints: 前半句写表面现象，后半句写你更确定的结论
templates.exampleSentence: It seems that the process is slow, but there is no doubt that it is effective.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这个过程看起来很慢，但毫无疑问它是有效的。

templates.label: 书面论证模板
templates.template: There is no doubt that + [结论], because + [原因].
slotHints: 先下明确结论，再补充原因；because 后不要重复 that 框架
templates.exampleSentence: There is no doubt that public transport matters, because it reduces traffic and pollution.
templates.exampleTranslation: 毫无疑问，公共交通很重要，因为它能减少交通拥堵和污染。

items.title: it is important to / it is necessary to
items.pattern: it is important to... / it is necessary to...
items.coreExplanation: 这个句型用来客观说明“做某事很重要/有必要”。基本结构是 It is important to + 动词原形 或 It is necessary to + 动词原形。it放句首作形式主语，真正内容是后面的to do，常见于句首，后面可接for短语说明对象。
items.usageNote: 语气中性偏书面，常见于建议、说明、课堂写作、邮件和正式表达。important to 表示“重要、值得重视”，necessary to 表示“有必要、必须做”，语气更强。不太适合表达很强的个人情绪，也少用于非常随便的聊天。常单独成句，或后接 for sb. to do。初学者先安全使用“It is important to learn English.”这类简单结构。

slotSchema.label: adjective
slotSchema.hint: 这里通常放important或necessary。常见错误是后面直接接动词原形而不用to。例：It is important to rest.

slotSchema.label: action
slotSchema.hint: 放to+动词原形，表示要做的事。不要用doing或过去式。例：to get enough sleep

slotSchema.label: person affected
slotSchema.hint: 可放for+人，表示“对谁来说做这件事”。常见错误是用of sb.。例：It is necessary for students to ask questions.

commonErrors: It is important study English.｜错在important后面必须接to do，不可直接接动词原形。应改为：It is important to study English.
It is necessary doing exercise every day.｜错在这里要用to do，不用doing。应改为：It is necessary to do exercise every day.
It is important for improve your pronunciation.｜错在for后面不能直接接动词；for后应接人，再接to do。应改为：It is important to improve your pronunciation. 或 It is important for learners to improve their pronunciation.
Is important to be careful.｜错在英语句子通常需要形式主语it。应改为：It is important to be careful.
It is necessary to not worry.｜语法不算绝对错误，但初学者更自然的否定位置是not to do。应改为：It is necessary not to worry.
It is important that to arrive early.｜错在that从句和to do结构混用了。应改为：It is important to arrive early. 或 It is important that you arrive early.

contrasts.title: important to vs necessary to
contrasts.note: 结构都可用 It is + 形容词 + to do，但语气不同。important表示“重要”，necessary表示“有必要、必须”，更强、更正式。比较：It is important to rest.（休息很重要）/ It is necessary to rest now.（现在有必要休息）

contrasts.title: vs it is helpful/useful to
contrasts.note: 结构一样，都是 It is + adjective + to do。区别在于 helpful/useful 强调“有帮助/有用”，语气较轻；important/necessary 强调“重要性/必要性”。比较：It is helpful to take notes. / It is important to take notes.

contrasts.title: vs there is no need to
contrasts.note: 前者表示“应该重视/有必要做”，后者表示“没有必要做”。结构也不同：It is important to do...；There is no need to do...。比较：It is necessary to call him. / There is no need to call him now.

contrasts.title: vs I think...
contrasts.note: I think + 从句更主观，更像个人意见；It is important/necessary to... 更客观、更适合作文和正式说明。比较：I think we should save water. / It is important to save water.

contrasts.title: vs it is important that + clause
contrasts.note: to do后面接动作本身，更短更基础；that从句后面接完整主语，更适合明确谁去做。比较：It is important to arrive on time. / It is important that everyone arrive on time.

examples.sentence: It is important to drink enough water.
examples.translation: 喝足够的水很重要。
examples.note: 最基础、最安全的to do用法。

examples.sentence: To protect the environment, it is important to reduce plastic use.
examples.translation: 为了保护环境，减少塑料使用很重要。
examples.note: 前面可先放目的状语，核心句型仍在主句中。

examples.sentence: It is necessary for students to review their notes after class.
examples.translation: 学生有必要在课后复习笔记。
examples.note: 加入for+人，明确“对谁来说”有必要。

examples.sentence: It is important to be on time, but it is not always necessary to arrive very early.
examples.translation: 准时很重要，但不一定有必要到得非常早。
examples.note: 同句对比important与necessary的语气差别。

examples.sentence: In academic writing, it is important to use clear topic sentences.
examples.translation: 在学术写作中，使用清晰的主题句很重要。
examples.note: 偏书面、说明性强。

examples.sentence: It is necessary to turn off your phone during the meeting.
examples.translation: 开会时有必要关掉手机。
examples.note: 日常规则或提醒中很自然。

templates.label: Safest beginner template
templates.template: It is important to + 动词原形
slotHints: 直接放一个常见动作，如 study, rest, drink water；不要忘记to
templates.exampleSentence: It is important to get enough sleep.
templates.exampleTranslation: 得到充足睡眠很重要。

templates.label: Necessity template
templates.template: It is necessary to + 动词原形
slotHints: 用于“有必要、必须做”的事情，语气比important更强
templates.exampleSentence: It is necessary to check your email every day.
templates.exampleTranslation: 每天查看邮件是有必要的。

templates.label: Alternate-position template
templates.template: It is important for + 人 + to + 动词原形
slotHints: 先说“对谁来说”，再说动作；for后接人，不接动词
templates.exampleSentence: It is important for children to read every day.
templates.exampleTranslation: 孩子们每天阅读很重要。

templates.label: Writing-oriented template
templates.template: In + 领域/场景, it is important to + 动词原形
slotHints: 前面先交代场景，逗号后接主句；适合作文和说明文
templates.exampleSentence: In job interviews, it is important to be polite.
templates.exampleTranslation: 在求职面试中，礼貌很重要。

items.title: it is helpful to / it is useful to
items.pattern: it is helpful to... / it is useful to...
items.coreExplanation: 这个句型用来说明“做某事有帮助/有用”，语气像温和建议，不像important或necessary那么强。基本结构是It is helpful to + 动词原形 / It is useful to + 动词原形。it作形式主语，真正内容放在to后面，常放在句首；后面也常接for + 人，说明“对谁有帮助”。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，常见于学习建议、说明书、邮件、课堂发言和实用写作。helpful更偏“有帮助”，useful更偏“有实际用处”。不太适合表达强制、规则或紧急义务，那时用important/necessary更自然。句首最常见，后面常直接接to do；若加对象，多放在helpful/useful后：It is helpful for students to... 初学者先稳用“it is helpful/useful to + 动词原形”即可。

slotSchema.label: 形容词
slotSchema.hint: 只能用helpful或useful。常见错误是和importance混用成*it is helpful that... 例：It is useful to review notes.

slotSchema.label: 动作
slotSchema.hint: 这里放to + 动词原形，表示具体行动。不要用动名词*to reviewing或完整从句*to you review。例：to make a list

slotSchema.label: 对象
slotSchema.hint: 可用for + 人，放在helpful/useful后面。常见错误是用*to students。例：It is helpful for beginners to ask questions.

commonErrors: *It is helpful that review your notes.——错在helpful后面通常接to do，不直接接that从句。应改为It is helpful to review your notes.
*It is useful for review notes.——错在for后面不能直接接动词短语。应改为It is useful to review notes. 或 It is useful for students to review notes.
*It is helpful to reviewing new words.——错在to后要用动词原形，不用-ing。应改为It is helpful to review new words.
*It helpful to make a plan.——错在缺少be动词is。应改为It is helpful to make a plan.
*It is useful to students review before class.——错在对象和动作顺序不对。应改为It is useful for students to review before class.
*Is helpful to take notes.——正式句子里需要形式主语it。应改为It is helpful to take notes.

contrasts.title: it is important/necessary to
contrasts.note: 结构上都接to do，但important/necessary语气更强，常表示义务、关键性；helpful/useful只是建议或实用价值。比较：It is helpful to arrive early.＝早点到会更有帮助；It is necessary to arrive early.＝必须早点到。

contrasts.title: there is no need to
contrasts.note: 结构不同：there is no need to表示“没必要做”，意思相反；helpful/useful表示“做了有帮助/有用”。语气上前者是在减轻要求，后者是在给建议。比较：There is no need to print it. / It is useful to save a copy.

contrasts.title: it is good to
contrasts.note: 两者都可接to do，但good范围更宽，常带一般积极评价；helpful/useful更强调实际效果。场景上给学习方法或步骤建议时，helpful/useful更具体。比较：It is good to read. / It is helpful to read the instructions first.

contrasts.title: it helps to
contrasts.note: 结构上it helps to + 动词原形更口语、更直接；it is helpful to更书面、更完整。意思接近，但前者像经验总结，后者像中性建议。比较：It helps to take breaks. / It is helpful to take breaks.

contrasts.title: with / without for + person
contrasts.note: 结构差别在是否点明对象：It is helpful to ask questions. 只说行为本身；It is helpful for beginners to ask questions. 明确“对初学者有帮助”。写作中若想更清楚指出受益人，加for + 人更自然。

examples.sentence: It is helpful to review your notes after class.
examples.translation: 课后复习笔记是有帮助的。
examples.note: 最安全的基础用法：句首 + to do。

examples.sentence: To avoid mistakes, it is useful to check your work twice.
examples.translation: 为了避免错误，检查两遍作业是有用的。
examples.note: 前面可先放目的短语，主句里仍用it is useful to。

examples.sentence: It is helpful for new students to keep a vocabulary notebook.
examples.translation: 对新生来说，准备一个词汇本是有帮助的。
examples.note: 加入for + 人，明确谁受益。

examples.sentence: In report writing, it is useful to use clear headings.
examples.translation: 在报告写作中，使用清晰的小标题很有用。
examples.note: 较像说明文或写作指导语气。

examples.sentence: It is helpful to arrive early, but it is not necessary.
examples.translation: 早点到会有帮助，但不是必须的。
examples.note: 和necessary形成清楚对比。

examples.sentence: When you study at home, it is useful to turn off your phone.
examples.translation: 在家学习时，把手机关掉很有用。
examples.note: 日常场景里很自然，表达实用建议。

templates.label: 最稳妥初学者模板
templates.template: It is helpful/useful to + 动词原形.
slotHints: 先选helpful或useful；后面直接接to do，不要加that，不要用-ing。
templates.exampleSentence: It is helpful to make a study plan.
templates.exampleTranslation: 制订学习计划是有帮助的。

templates.label: 带对象模板
templates.template: It is helpful/useful for + 人 + to + 动词原形.
slotHints: 想说“对谁有帮助”时加for + 人；不要误用to + 人。
templates.exampleSentence: It is useful for beginners to practice every day.
templates.exampleTranslation: 对初学者来说，每天练习很有用。

templates.label: 前置场景模板
templates.template: In/When + 场景, it is helpful/useful to + 动词原形.
slotHints: 先交代场景，再给建议；逗号后接完整主句。
templates.exampleSentence: In group work, it is helpful to share roles clearly.
templates.exampleTranslation: 在小组合作中，明确分工是有帮助的。

templates.label: 写作说明模板
templates.template: To + 目的, it is useful to + 动词原形.
slotHints: 前面用To + 目的说明“为了……”，后面给出实用做法。
templates.exampleSentence: To save time, it is useful to prepare the materials early.
templates.exampleTranslation: 为了节省时间，提前准备材料很有用。

items.title: it is hard to / it is easy to
items.pattern: it is hard to... / it is easy to...
items.coreExplanation: 这个句型用来评价“做某事”容易还是困难：It is hard to... / It is easy to...。真正要做的动作放在to后面，用动词原形；it只是形式主语。最常见放在句首先给出判断，后面可接for人说明“对谁来说”。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，口语、课堂表达、说明文和基础议论文都很常用，适合谈学习、生活技能、理解规则等。它强调“某动作本身难/易”，不适合直接表达“我做得很吃力”的个人经历，那时常用have trouble + -ing。句首最自然；若前面已有长主语，也可后置但初学者优先用“It is hard/easy to...”安全模板。句号前直接结束，不要在to后再接完整从句。

slotSchema.label: difficulty word
slotSchema.hint: 通常放hard或easy，也可换成difficult等近义词。常见错误是写成hardly；hard是“困难的”，hardly是“几乎不”。例：It is hard to choose.

slotSchema.label: action after to
slotSchema.hint: 这里放to + 动词原形，可带宾语，如to understand this rule。常见错误是用to understanding或to understands。例：It is easy to make mistakes.

slotSchema.label: optional for + person
slotSchema.hint: 需要说明“对谁来说”时，用for + 人，通常放hard/easy后、to前。常见错误是用of + 人；这里评价难度通常用for。例：It is hard for beginners to remember them.

commonErrors: Wrong: It is hard understand this rule.｜错因：to后缺少动词不定式标记。｜改法：It is hard to understand this rule.
Wrong: It is hard to understanding this rule.｜错因：to后要用动词原形，不用-ing。｜改法：It is hard to understand this rule.
Wrong: It is easy that learn English.｜错因：easy/hard后这里不接that从句来表示动作难易。｜改法：It is easy to learn English.
Wrong: I am hard to finish this work.｜错因：hard描述“人”会变成别的意思，这里应评价“做这件事”本身。｜改法：It is hard to finish this work.
Wrong: It is hard of me to answer.｜错因：表示“对某人来说难”通常用for，不用of。｜改法：It is hard for me to answer.
Wrong: It is hardly to see the screen.｜错因：hardly意为“几乎不”，不能替代hard。｜改法：It is hard to see the screen.

contrasts.title: have trouble + -ing
contrasts.note: 结构不同：have trouble后接动词-ing；it is hard to后接to + 动词原形。语气上，have trouble更像说“实际遇到困难”，更个人、更经历化；it is hard to更像一般判断。对比：I have trouble sleeping. / It is hard to sleep here.

contrasts.title: it is important to / it is necessary to
contrasts.note: 结构相似，都是It is + 形容词 + to...；但important/necessary是在说“重要、必要”，不是“难易”。场景上前者常用于建议、规则、正式写作；hard/easy用于能力、任务难度。对比：It is important to practice. / It is hard to practice every day.

contrasts.title: for + person vs of + person
contrasts.note: 结构区别：It is hard/easy for + 人 + to...；而It is kind/smart of + 人 + to...。for强调“对谁来说难/易”，of评价人的性格或品质。不要混用。对比：It is hard for me to say no. / It is kind of you to help me.

contrasts.title: too...to...
contrasts.note: too + 形容词 + to...表示“太……而不能……”，结果更强、更消极；it is hard to只是说“难”，不一定做不到。场景上too...to更强调程度。对比：It is hard to read this. / It is too small to read.

contrasts.title: hard vs hardly
contrasts.note: 形式接近但意义完全不同：hard是“困难的/努力地”，hardly是“几乎不”。在本句型里只能用hard，不用hardly。最小对比：It is hard to hear. / I can hardly hear you.

examples.sentence: It is hard to learn a new language.
examples.translation: 学习一门新语言是困难的。
examples.note: 最基础、安全的核心句型。

examples.sentence: It is easy for children to pick up new words.
examples.translation: 对孩子来说，学会新单词很容易。
examples.note: 展示for + 人的位置：easy后，to前。

examples.sentence: For many students, it is hard to stay focused for a long time.
examples.translation: 对许多学生来说，长时间保持专注很难。
examples.note: 前面先放背景短语，主句仍用it is hard to。

examples.sentence: In modern life, it is easy to get information but hard to judge whether it is reliable.
examples.translation: 在现代生活中，获取信息容易，但判断它是否可靠很难。
examples.note: 适合写作中的对比表达。

examples.sentence: It is hard to say which plan is better.
examples.translation: 很难说哪个计划更好。
examples.note: 常见固定用法，后面可接间接问句。

examples.sentence: It is easy to make this mistake when you are tired.
examples.translation: 你累的时候很容易犯这个错误。
examples.note: 日常提醒中很自然。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
templates.template: It is hard/easy to + 动词原形 + 其他.
slotHints: 先选hard或easy；to后放动作，如 learn English / find the answer。不要用-ing。
templates.exampleSentence: It is easy to find this station.
templates.exampleTranslation: 找到这个车站很容易。

templates.label: 带对象模板
templates.template: It is hard/easy for + 人 + to + 动词原形 + 其他.
slotHints: for后放对谁来说，如 for me / for beginners；不要换成of。
templates.exampleSentence: It is hard for beginners to remember all these words.
templates.exampleTranslation: 对初学者来说，记住所有这些单词很难。

templates.label: 前置场景模板
templates.template: In/For + 场景, it is hard/easy to + 动词原形 + 其他.
slotHints: 前面先交代范围或场景，如 In daily life, For many people；逗号后接主句。
templates.exampleSentence: In daily life, it is hard to avoid stress.
templates.exampleTranslation: 在日常生活中，很难避免压力。

templates.label: 写作对比模板
templates.template: It is easy to + A, but hard to + B.
slotHints: A和B用两个可比较的动作，形成清楚对比。
templates.exampleSentence: It is easy to copy information, but hard to understand it deeply.
templates.exampleTranslation: 复制信息很容易，但深入理解它很难。

items.title: it is common for... to...
items.pattern: it is common for + noun + to + base
items.coreExplanation: It is common for + 名词/人群 + to do... 用来客观说明“某人或某类事物常常会做某事”。for 后面通常放人、群体或事物，to 后面接动词原形。整句常放在句首作总体判断，后面可接具体说明。
items.usageNote: 语气较中性偏书面，常见于说明文、分析、课堂写作、报告中，用来讲“普遍现象”而不是个人意见。日常聊天也能用，但会比 people often... 更正式。若只说一次、偶然情况，或你不确定真假，不宜用它。通常整句放句首，后面可加逗号再补充细节。初学者最安全的做法是先写 It is common for people/students/parents to...

slotSchema.label: for后面的对象
slotSchema.hint: 放人、群体或事物，如 people, students, parents, children。不要直接放动词或完整句子。误：for study hard；正：for students to study hard.

slotSchema.label: to后面的动作
slotSchema.hint: 用 to + 动词原形，如 to make mistakes, to feel tired。不要用动名词或三单。误：to making / to makes；正：to make.

slotSchema.label: 补充情境
slotSchema.hint: 可加时间、地点、条件等短语，如 in big cities, during exams。不要把它插进 for 和 to 中间。正：It is common for students to feel stressed during exams.

commonErrors: 误：It is common for students study late. 原因：to 不可省略，后面必须接动词原形。改：It is common for students to study late.
误：It is common for students to studying late. 原因：to 后不能接 -ing。改：It is common for students to study late.
误：It is common that students to study late. 原因：这个句型不用 common that... to...。改：It is common for students to study late. 或 It is common that students study late（但前者更常见、更自然）。
误：It is common for study late. 原因：for 后需要名词性对象，说明“谁/什么常这样”。改：It is common for students to study late.
误：Students are common to study late. 原因：common 不能这样直接描述“学生做某事”。改：It is common for students to study late.
误：In big cities, it is common for people often to eat out. 原因：often 与 common 都表示频率，容易重复且位置别扭。改：In big cities, it is common for people to eat out. 或 People in big cities often eat out.

contrasts.title: people often...
contrasts.note: 结构上是主语 + often + 动词，更口语直接；it is common for... to... 是形式主语结构，更客观、更书面。日常聊天更常说 People often eat out.；写分析更自然：It is common for people to eat out in big cities.

contrasts.title: it seems that...
contrasts.note: 结构上 seems 后接 that 从句；common for... to... 后接 for + 名词 + to do。语气上 seems 表示“看起来、推测”，common 表示“普遍现象”。对比：It seems that he is tired.（看起来）/ It is common for students to feel tired during finals.（普遍）

contrasts.title: one of the most ... is ...
contrasts.note: one of the most... 用来指出“最……之一”，强调突出项目；common for... to... 讲常见行为，不强调排名。对比：One of the most common problems is stress. / It is common for students to feel stressed.

contrasts.title: it is normal for... to...
contrasts.note: 两者结构接近，都是 for + 名词 + to do；但 normal 更像“正常、合理”，带评价色彩，common 只说“常见”。对比：It is normal for babies to cry.（正常）/ It is common for tourists to take photos here.（常见）

contrasts.title: according to...
contrasts.note: according to + 来源，表示“根据……”；it is common for... to... 不需要信息来源，直接陈述一般规律。正式写作中若有依据，可连用：According to the survey, it is common for young people to shop online.

examples.sentence: It is common for students to make mistakes.
examples.translation: 学生犯错是很常见的。
examples.note: 最安全的基础句型：for 后放群体，to 后放动作。

examples.sentence: In big cities, it is common for people to eat out.
examples.translation: 在大城市里，人们在外面吃饭很常见。
examples.note: 前面先放地点短语，是常见的变体位置。

examples.sentence: It is common for consumers to compare prices before buying.
examples.translation: 消费者在购买前比较价格是很常见的。
examples.note: 较适合说明文、报告、图表作文。

examples.sentence: It is common for new learners to feel nervous, but it is normal for them to improve slowly.
examples.translation: 新学习者感到紧张很常见，但他们进步较慢也是正常的。
examples.note: 这一句可帮助区分 common 与 normal。

examples.sentence: It is common for parents to worry about their children.
examples.translation: 父母为孩子担心是很常见的。
examples.note: 日常交流也能用，只是语气比口语稍正式。

examples.sentence: According to the report, it is common for young adults to change jobs in their twenties.
examples.translation: 根据这份报告，二十多岁的年轻人换工作是很常见的。
examples.note: 和 according to 连用时很自然，适合正式写作。

templates.label: 基础安全模板
templates.template: It is common for [群体] to [动词原形].
slotHints: [群体] 放 people/students/parents 等名词短语
[动词原形] 用 study, worry, make mistakes 等原形
templates.exampleSentence: It is common for children to ask questions.
templates.exampleTranslation: 孩子提问是很常见的。

templates.label: 前置情境模板
templates.template: [时间/地点], it is common for [群体] to [动词原形].
slotHints: 前面放 in big cities, during exams, in winter 等情境
主句仍保持 for + 名词 + to + 动词原形
templates.exampleSentence: During exams, it is common for students to feel stressed.
templates.exampleTranslation: 考试期间，学生感到有压力是很常见的。

templates.label: 写作说明模板
templates.template: According to [来源], it is common for [群体] to [动词原形].
slotHints: [来源] 可用 the report, the survey, the data
适合说明文、议论文，不适合随便编造来源
