templates.exampleSentence: According to the survey, it is common for commuters to spend over an hour on the road.
templates.exampleTranslation: 根据调查，通勤者在路上花一个多小时是很常见的。

templates.label: 对比补充模板
templates.template: It is common for [群体] to [动词原形], especially [情境].
slotHints: especially 后补充更具体的场景
不要把 especially 放到 for 和 to 中间
templates.exampleSentence: It is common for office workers to skip breakfast, especially on busy weekdays.
templates.exampleTranslation: 上班族不吃早餐很常见，尤其是在忙碌的工作日。

items.title: one of the most ... is ...
items.pattern: one of the most + adjective + nouns is...
items.coreExplanation: 这个结构用来把某个事物放进“最……的……之一”这个大类里，再指出它是什么或为什么重要，表示“它是其中非常突出的一个”。基本形式是 one of the most + 形容词 + 复数名词 + is + 名词/短语/that从句。它常放在句首作主语，后面接 is。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，常见于作文、介绍、演讲、说明重要原因或特点时，听起来比 very important 更有层次。适合写“教育、环境、健康、城市问题”等主题。不太适合随口聊天里反复使用，否则会显得书面、笨重。one 前面一般不加 the；most 后面要接形容词，名词必须用复数。整串通常在句首，后面直接接 is，不要随便拆开。初学者先安全套用“one of the most important + 复数名词 + is + 名词”。

slotSchema.label: 形容词
slotSchema.hint: 放表示评价的形容词，如 important, serious, useful。常见错误是把副词放这里，如 very importance；应说 most important。例：most important

slotSchema.label: 复数名词
slotSchema.hint: 放可数复数名词，表示“大类”，如 problems, reasons, skills。常见错误是用单数，如 reason；应说 reasons。例：the most important reasons

slotSchema.label: is后内容
slotSchema.hint: 可放名词短语、疑问词短语或 that 从句，如 is education / is how we use time / is that... 常见错误是 is 后只放动词原形。例：is communication

commonErrors: one of the most important reason is...：reason 用了单数。one of 后面通常接复数名词。应改为 one of the most important reasons is...
one of most important reasons is...：缺少 the。固定说法是 one of the most + 形容词 + 复数名词。应改为 one of the most important reasons is...
one of the most importantly reasons is...：important 误写成副词 importantly。most 后面这里要接形容词。应改为 one of the most important reasons is...
one of the most important reasons are...：主语中心是 one，所以谓语通常用单数 is，不用 are。应改为 one of the most important reasons is...
the one of the most important reasons is...：一般不用 the one of。若表示其中之一，直接说 one of the most...；若特指某一个，可重写整句。应改为 one of the most important reasons is...
one of the most important reasons is protect the environment：is 后面直接接动词原形不自然。应改为 is to protect the environment，或更自然地说 is environmental protection / is that we should protect the environment

contrasts.title: very important vs one of the most...
contrasts.note: 结构不同：very important 只是普通评价；one of the most + 复数名词 是“在一类中排名很高的一个”。语气上前者更口语，后者更适合作文和正式说明。对比：Exercise is very important. / Exercise is one of the most important habits.

contrasts.title: the most ... vs one of the most ...
contrasts.note: 结构不同：the most 表示“最……的那一个”；one of the most 表示“最……的一类中的一个”，语气更稳妥。场景上，前者更绝对，后者更适合避免说得太满。对比：This is the most important reason. / This is one of the most important reasons.

contrasts.title: what matters is...
contrasts.note: 结构不同：what matters is + 内容，直接指出“关键在于什么”；one of the most ... is... 先建立一个类别，再举出其中一个重要项。前者更聚焦结论，后者更适合分类展开。对比：What matters is practice. / One of the most important skills is practice.

contrasts.title: it is common for... to...
contrasts.note: 结构不同：it is common for A to do B 说“某事常见”；one of the most ... is... 说“某项很重要/很突出”。语义和场景不同，不能互换。对比：It is common for students to feel stressed. / One of the most serious problems is stress.

examples.sentence: One of the most important reasons is education.
examples.translation: 最重要的原因之一是教育。
examples.note: 最安全的入门句型：is 后接名词。

examples.sentence: For many students, one of the most useful skills is time management.
examples.translation: 对许多学生来说，最有用的技能之一是时间管理。
examples.note: 前面可先加范围短语，核心结构仍保持完整。

examples.sentence: One of the most serious problems in modern cities is air pollution.
examples.translation: 现代城市中最严重的问题之一是空气污染。
examples.note: 常用于书面说明社会话题。

examples.sentence: One of the most important differences is how people use their free time.
examples.translation: 最重要的差异之一是人们如何使用空闲时间。
examples.note: is 后也可以接疑问词引导的内容。

examples.sentence: One of the most effective ways to stay healthy is regular exercise.
examples.translation: 保持健康最有效的方法之一是规律运动。
examples.note: 说明“方法”时很自然。

examples.sentence: One of the most enjoyable parts of travel is meeting new people.
examples.translation: 旅行中最令人享受的部分之一是认识新朋友。
examples.note: 日常主题也能用，但语气比口语稍书面。

templates.label: 安全初学者模板
templates.template: One of the most [adj] [pluralNoun] is [complement].
slotHints: [adj] 放 important/useful/common 等评价词
[pluralNoun] 用复数名词，如 reasons/problems/skills
[complement] 先放名词最安全，如 education/time management
templates.exampleSentence: One of the most important reasons is communication.
templates.exampleTranslation: 最重要的原因之一是沟通。

templates.label: 带范围的模板
templates.template: For [group], one of the most [adj] [pluralNoun] is [complement].
slotHints: [group] 放人群，如 students/parents
后面核心结构不要改，仍然用 is
适合先说明“对谁来说”
templates.exampleSentence: For teenagers, one of the most important skills is self-control.
templates.exampleTranslation: 对青少年来说，最重要的技能之一是自控力。

templates.label: 写作用模板
templates.template: One of the most [adj] [pluralNoun] in [topic] is [complement].
slotHints: [topic] 放范围，如 modern life/city life/education
适合社会、教育、环境类作文
注意 in 后是范围，不是完整句子
templates.exampleSentence: One of the most serious problems in city life is noise pollution.
templates.exampleTranslation: 城市生活中最严重的问题之一是噪音污染。

items.title: the best way to... is to...
items.pattern: the best way to + goal + is to + action
items.coreExplanation: 这个结构用来直接提出“实现某个目标的最佳方法”。基本形式是the best way to+目标+is to+动作；前半部分写想达到的事，后半部分写具体做法。整句常放在句首作主句，也常用于回答“怎么做最好”这类问题。
items.usageNote: 语气直接、清楚，属neutral，口语和写作都很常用，尤其适合给建议、说明步骤、写议论文观点。它强调“最佳方法”，所以适合谈一般做法或经验，不太适合绝对化、无法证明的场景。通常整句完整出现；若放句中，前后结构也要完整。初学者最安全的做法是两边都用to do，不要一边用名词一边随意换时态。

slotSchema.label: 目标
slotSchema.hint: 这里放想实现的事，最常见是to+动词，如to learn English。不要只放裸动词learn English。例：to stay healthy

slotSchema.label: 做法
slotSchema.hint: 这里放具体行动，用to+动词，如to exercise every day。不要写成exercising every day。例：to ask your teacher for help

slotSchema.label: 补充信息
slotSchema.hint: 可在action后加方式、时间、对象等，如in class, every day。不要插在the best way和to之间。例：to practice with friends every day

commonErrors: Wrong: The best way improve English is to read more.｜错因：way后面通常要接to do表示目标。｜改法：The best way to improve English is to read more.
Wrong: The best way to improve English is reading more.｜错因：这张卡的基础安全格式是is to do，前后更整齐自然。｜改法：The best way to improve English is to read more.
Wrong: The best way for improve English is to read more.｜错因：for不能直接代替这里的to。｜改法：The best way to improve English is to read more.
Wrong: The best way to improving English is to read more.｜错因：to后应接动词原形，不接V-ing。｜改法：The best way to improve English is to read more.
Wrong: The best way to improve English is read more.｜错因：be动词后少了to，不符合本卡固定框架。｜改法：The best way to improve English is to read more.
Wrong: The best way to improve English, is to read more.｜错因：主语和be动词之间一般不要随便加逗号。｜改法：The best way to improve English is to read more.

contrasts.title: it is important to...
contrasts.note: 结构不同：It is important to+动作，只说“重要”；本结构是The best way to+目标+is to+做法，强调“最佳方法”。语气上前者较泛，后者更像给方案。对比：It is important to practice. / The best way to improve is to practice daily.

contrasts.title: it is necessary to...
contrasts.note: 结构上necessary to表示“有必要做”，不直接回答“怎么做最好”；best way...is to...明确给出方法。场景上前者常见于规则、要求，后者常见于建议。对比：It is necessary to sleep enough. / The best way to feel better is to sleep enough.

contrasts.title: what matters is...
contrasts.note: 结构上What matters is+内容，强调关键点；The best way to... is to...强调实现目标的手段。语气上前者更抽象，后者更操作性。对比：What matters is consistency. / The best way to improve is to practice every day.

contrasts.title: How can I...?
contrasts.note: How can I...?是提问；The best way to... is to...是回答或建议。场景上前者用于求助，后者用于给方案。最小对比：How can I save money? / The best way to save money is to make a budget.

contrasts.title: a good way to...
contrasts.note: 结构相近，但a good way to...只是“一个不错的方法”，语气更保守；the best way to...更强，表示最推荐。若你不确定是否真是“最佳”，可用a good way更自然。对比：A good way to relax is to walk. / The best way to relax is to walk alone.

examples.sentence: The best way to learn English is to practice every day.
examples.translation: 学英语最好的方法是每天练习。
examples.note: 最核心、最安全的基础句型。

examples.sentence: For beginners, the best way to remember new words is to use them in sentences.
examples.translation: 对初学者来说，记住生词的最好方法是在句子里使用它们。
examples.note: 前面可加短语说明对象，主结构不变。

examples.sentence: The best way to reduce traffic in big cities is to improve public transportation.
examples.translation: 减少大城市交通拥堵的最佳方法是改善公共交通。
examples.note: 较适合说明文、议论文写作。

examples.sentence: The best way to stay healthy is to exercise regularly, not to skip meals.
examples.translation: 保持健康的最好方法是规律锻炼，而不是不吃饭。
examples.note: 和其他做法比较时很自然。

examples.sentence: The best way to solve this problem is to talk to your teacher first.
examples.translation: 解决这个问题最好的办法是先和你的老师谈谈。
examples.note: 日常给建议很常用。

examples.sentence: The best way to save money is to cook at home more often.
examples.translation: 省钱最好的方法是更常在家做饭。
examples.note: 生活场景中的自然表达。

templates.label: 最安全基础模板
templates.template: The best way to [goal] is to [action].
slotHints: [goal]放想实现的目标，用to+动词，如 to improve pronunciation
[action]放具体做法，用to+动词，如 to listen and repeat every day
templates.exampleSentence: The best way to improve pronunciation is to listen and repeat every day.
templates.exampleTranslation: 提高发音最好的方法是每天听并跟读。

templates.label: 带对象/范围的模板
templates.template: For [person/group], the best way to [goal] is to [action].
slotHints: [person/group]放适用对象，如 beginners, students, busy workers
[goal]仍然用to+动词；[action]写最推荐的做法
templates.exampleSentence: For busy students, the best way to manage time is to make a weekly plan.
templates.exampleTranslation: 对忙碌的学生来说，管理时间最好的方法是做一个每周计划。

templates.label: 写作用模板
templates.template: The best way to [solve/achieve/reduce] [issue] is to [action].
slotHints: [solve/achieve/reduce]选和议题搭配的动词，如 solve a problem, reduce waste
[issue]放问题或目标；[action]写政策、措施或步骤
templates.exampleSentence: The best way to reduce waste is to reuse things whenever possible.
templates.exampleTranslation: 减少浪费的最佳方法是在可能的时候重复使用物品。

items.title: what matters is...
items.pattern: what matters is + noun / clause
items.coreExplanation: what matters is... 用来把注意力拉到“最重要的事”上，意思接近“关键是……”。固定框架通常是 What matters is + 名词短语/that从句/to do。它常放在句首先立主题，也可放在较长句后半部分作总结。
items.usageNote: 语气多为中性，也常见于讨论、劝说、说明理由和英语写作中，能帮助你从细节转向重点。适合表达原则、判断标准、真正重要的因素；不适合只是在随便聊天时连续反复使用，否则会显得太用力。what matters 后面先接 is，再接内容；若放句首，后面通常直接继续，不必加逗号。初学者最安全的做法是先用 What matters is + 名词短语。

slotSchema.label: 核心内容
slotSchema.hint: 这里放真正重要的内容：可用名词短语、that从句或to do。常错：直接放完整独立句却不加that。例：What matters is honesty. / What matters is that we try.

slotSchema.label: 讨论对象
slotSchema.hint: 可在前句先交代讨论对象，再用本结构点出重点。常错：把对象硬塞进 what matters 后面造成句子过长。例：In interviews, what matters is preparation.

slotSchema.label: 要做的事
slotSchema.hint: 若重点是行动，可用 to do。常错：误用 doing 在这里当主要补语。例：What matters is to stay calm.

commonErrors: What matters that we try.｜错因：what matters 后面必须先有系动词 is。｜改法：What matters is that we try.
What matter is honesty.｜错因：matter 在这里主语是 what，谓语要用第三人称单数 matters。｜改法：What matters is honesty.
What matters is we try.｜错因：口语里偶尔能听到，但初学者这样写不稳妥，正式写作尤其不推荐。｜改法：What matters is that we try.
What matters are honesty.｜错因：固定框架通常用单数 is，别被后面的名词迷惑。｜改法：What matters is honesty.
What matters is not money, but being happy is.｜错因：后半句重复 is，结构不平行。｜改法：What matters is not money but happiness. / What matters is being happy, not money.
In my opinion what matters, is trust.｜错因：what matters 和 is 之间不要随便断开加逗号。｜改法：In my opinion, what matters is trust.

contrasts.title: the fact is that...
contrasts.note: 结构上 the fact is that + 从句；what matters is + 重点内容。前者是“事实是”，强调真实情况；后者是“关键是”，强调重要性。比较：The fact is that we are late.（事实）/ What matters is that we arrive safely.（重点）

contrasts.title: the best way to... is to...
contrasts.note: 结构上 the best way to X is to Y 更具体，谈“最佳方法”；what matters is 更宽，可谈原则、标准、价值。语气上前者像给方案，后者像拉回重点。比较：The best way to learn is to practice. / What matters is regular practice.

contrasts.title: I think that...
contrasts.note: 结构上 I think (that) + 观点；what matters is + 重点。前者明确是“我的看法”，语气更主观；后者更像总结或定标准，常用于讨论和写作。比较：I think this plan is fine. / What matters is whether it works.

contrasts.title: in my opinion...
contrasts.note: 结构上 In my opinion, + 句子；what matters is + 名词/从句/to do。前者是加一个“依我看”的立场标记，后者是把焦点放在关键点上。可连用但功能不同：In my opinion, what matters is trust.

contrasts.title: what matters most is...
contrasts.note: 结构上只多了 most，语义更强，表示“最重要的是”。语气上比 what matters is 更强调排序。若只是指出关键点，用普通形式更自然。比较：What matters is honesty. / What matters most is honesty.

examples.sentence: What matters is honesty.
examples.translation: 重要的是诚实。
examples.note: 最安全的基础句型：is 后面接名词短语。

examples.sentence: In group work, what matters is that everyone understands the goal.
examples.translation: 在小组合作中，重要的是每个人都明白目标。
examples.note: 前面先交代场景，再用本结构点重点。

examples.sentence: What matters is to keep the argument clear and consistent.
examples.translation: 重要的是让论证保持清楚且一致。
examples.note: 写作中可用 to do 强调行动目标。

examples.sentence: The fact is that the task is difficult, but what matters is whether we can finish it on time.
examples.translation: 事实是这个任务很难，但关键是我们能否按时完成。
examples.note: 和 the fact is that 对比：前者讲事实，后者讲关键点。

examples.sentence: Don’t worry too much about the price—what matters is quality.
examples.translation: 别太担心价格——关键是质量。
examples.note: 很自然的日常提醒用法。

examples.sentence: When choosing a roommate, what matters most is trust, not shared hobbies.
examples.translation: 选室友时，最重要的是信任，而不是共同爱好。
examples.note: 展示与 what matters most is 的细微强弱区别。

templates.label: 安全基础模板
templates.template: What matters is + [核心名词短语].
slotHints: 这里放最重要的事，如 honesty, trust, quality。避免放完整句子。
templates.exampleSentence: What matters is clear communication.
templates.exampleTranslation: 重要的是清晰的沟通。

templates.label: 从句模板
templates.template: What matters is that + [完整句子].
slotHints: 这里放一个完整意思，如 we keep trying。不要省略 that。
templates.exampleSentence: What matters is that we keep trying.
templates.exampleTranslation: 重要的是我们继续努力。

templates.label: 场景前置模板
templates.template: In/When + [场景], what matters is + [重点].
slotHints: 前半部分写场景，如 in class, when traveling；后半部分写关键因素。
templates.exampleSentence: When traveling, what matters is safety.
templates.exampleTranslation: 旅行时，重要的是安全。

templates.label: 写作导向模板
templates.template: What matters is to + [动词原形] + ...
slotHints: 用于强调该做什么，如 to stay calm, to present evidence clearly。
templates.exampleSentence: What matters is to present your ideas clearly.
templates.exampleTranslation: 重要的是清楚地表达你的想法。

items.title: one reason is that... another reason is that...
items.pattern: one reason is that... another reason is that...
items.coreExplanation: 这个结构用来把多个理由分条说清楚，常见形式是“One reason is that + 完整句子. Another reason is that + 完整句子.”。that后面通常接完整陈述句，不接单个名词。它常放在句首或某个观点句之后，特别适合写作中有条理地说明原因。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，偏书面，但口语中慢慢解释理由时也能用。适合英语作文、邮件、回答“Why...?”、做简短陈述。不太适合特别随意的闲聊，也不适合只说一个理由时机械地硬套两段。通常每个reason后单独成句，句号最安全；也可先说结论，再接这个框架。初学者记住最稳妥做法：reason后面用is that，that后面写完整主谓句。

slotSchema.label: 第一个理由内容
slotSchema.hint: 这里放完整陈述句，要有主语和谓语。常见错误是只放名词或短语，如“the price”；应改成“the price is low”。例：it saves time

slotSchema.label: 第二个理由内容
slotSchema.hint: 也放完整陈述句，最好和前一句并列、信息独立。常见错误是和前句重复或只写形容词，如“convenient”；应改成“it is convenient for students”。例：it is easy to use

slotSchema.label: 前置观点（可选）
slotSchema.hint: 可以先写你的观点，再列理由。常见错误是观点太长导致结构松散；建议一句话后接理由。例：I prefer studying at home

commonErrors: One reason is because it is cheap. → 错在把is that结构和because混在一起了。改成One reason is that it is cheap. 或 One reason is because it is cheap. 但本卡安全写法用is that。
One reason is that low price. → 错在that后面不是完整句子，只有名词短语。改成One reason is that the price is low.
Another reason that it is convenient. → 错在少了be动词，固定框架应是Another reason is that...。改成Another reason is that it is convenient.
One reason is that it is cheap, another reason is that convenient. → 错在第二个that后仍缺完整句子。改成..., another reason is that it is convenient. 初学者更安全：分成两个句子。
The reason is that it is cheap. Another reason is that it is convenient. → 不算绝对错误，但前后框架不平行，列举感较弱。若要明确列两点，改成One reason is that... Another reason is that...
One reason is that it saves time. The another reason is that it is fun. → 错在another前一般不用the。改成Another reason is that it is fun.

contrasts.title: for one thing..., for another...
contrasts.note: 结构上常是“For one thing, 句子; for another, 句子.”，不需要is that。语气更自然、更口语一点；“one reason is that...”更明白、更像写作框架。对比：One reason is that it is cheap. / For one thing, it is cheap.

contrasts.title: the reason is that...
contrasts.note: 结构上“The reason is that...”通常回答单一原因；“One reason is that...”是在多个原因中列出其中一个。语气上前者更像定义真正原因，后者更像条理列点。对比：The reason is that I was sick. / One reason is that I was sick.

contrasts.title: because...
contrasts.note: 结构上because直接引导原因从句，常跟在主句后；“one reason is that...”是先搭框架再给原因。because更口语、更直接；本结构更适合组织段落。对比：I stayed home because it rained. / One reason I stayed home is that it rained.

contrasts.title: first..., second...
contrasts.note: 结构上first/second是顺序标记，不一定表示“理由”；“one reason... another reason...”明确表示原因。语气上first/second更像列点提纲，后者更解释性。对比：First, it is cheap. Second, it is safe. / One reason is that it is cheap. Another reason is that it is safe.

contrasts.title: One reason for + 名词 is that...
contrasts.note: 结构上可说“One reason for this choice is that...”，中间多了for + 名词；本卡核心结构更短。前者更正式、书面，适合作文；后者更基础、好上手。对比：One reason for my decision is that it saves money. / One reason is that it saves money.

examples.sentence: One reason is that it is cheap. Another reason is that it is easy to use.
examples.translation: 一个原因是它很便宜。另一个原因是它很容易使用。
examples.note: 最基础的双理由模板，两个that后都是完整句子。

examples.sentence: I like online classes. One reason is that they save time. Another reason is that I can study at home.
examples.translation: 我喜欢网课。一个原因是它们能节省时间。另一个原因是我可以在家学习。
examples.note: 先给观点，再接理由框架，很常见。

examples.sentence: One reason is that public transport reduces traffic. Another reason is that it is better for the environment.
examples.translation: 一个原因是公共交通能减少交通拥堵。另一个原因是它对环境更好。
examples.note: 偏写作、说明文风格。

examples.sentence: One reason is that the apartment is cheaper. Another reason is that it is closer to my office.
examples.translation: 一个原因是这套公寓更便宜。另一个原因是它离我的办公室更近。
examples.note: 展示比较级内容；that后仍然是完整句子。

examples.sentence: I want to join the club. One reason is that I can make new friends. Another reason is that the activities look fun.
examples.translation: 我想加入这个社团。一个原因是我能认识新朋友。另一个原因是这些活动看起来很有趣。
examples.note: 日常校园场景很自然。

examples.sentence: One reason is that the report was finished late. Another reason is that some data were missing.
examples.translation: 一个原因是报告完成得晚。另一个原因是一些数据缺失了。
examples.note: 较书面，适合解释问题原因。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
templates.template: One reason is that {reason1_clause}. Another reason is that {reason2_clause}.
slotHints: 两个空格都填完整句子，不要只填名词或形容词。
templates.exampleSentence: One reason is that the course is useful. Another reason is that the teacher explains clearly.
templates.exampleTranslation: 一个原因是这门课很有用。另一个原因是老师讲解得很清楚。

templates.label: 先观点后理由模板
templates.template: {opening_claim}. One reason is that {reason1_clause}. Another reason is that {reason2_clause}.
slotHints: 第一句先写你的立场或选择，后面两句分别给理由。
templates.exampleSentence: I prefer city life. One reason is that it is more convenient. Another reason is that there are more job opportunities.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我更喜欢城市生活。一个原因是它更方便。另一个原因是那里有更多工作机会。

templates.label: 写作用更完整模板
templates.template: One reason for this {fixed_chunk} is that {reason1_clause}. Another reason is that {reason2_clause}.
slotHints: {fixed_chunk}可用change/decision/problem/choice等名词，让句子更像作文。
templates.exampleSentence: One reason for this decision is that it will save money. Another reason is that it will reduce stress.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这个决定的一个原因是它会省钱。另一个原因是它会减轻压力。

items.title: for this reason, ...
items.pattern: for this reason, statement
items.coreExplanation: for this reason 用来把前面已经说明的原因、事实或分析，连接到后面的结论、判断或做法，意思接近“因此、基于这个原因”。后面通常直接接完整陈述句，常放在句首并加逗号，也可放句中承接上文。
items.usageNote: 这个表达语气偏正式但仍很清楚，最自然的场景是作文、报告、说明理由、课堂发言和较完整的书面表达。不太适合非常随意的聊天；口语里更常说 so 或 that’s why。它通常放在新句开头，后面常接逗号；不要单独放句尾。初学者最安全的做法是：先写原因句，再另起一句用 For this reason, + 结论。

slotSchema.label: 固定连接语
slotSchema.hint: 固定写 for this reason。常见错误是漏掉 this 或写成 for this reasons。例：For this reason, we changed the plan.

slotSchema.label: 结论或结果句
slotSchema.hint: 这里放完整陈述句，要有主语和谓语。常见错误是后面只接名词短语。例：For this reason, the school changed the rule.

commonErrors: For this reason, because the road was closed.｜错在后面不是完整结论句，而是 another reason 从句。应改为 For this reason, we took another road. 或 Because the road was closed, we took another road.
For this reasons, we stayed home.｜错在固定搭配，reason 要单数。应改为 For this reason, we stayed home.
We stayed home for this reason the storm was coming.｜错在把它放句中却不做停顿，后面结构也不完整。应改为 We stayed home. For this reason, we canceled the trip. 或 We stayed home because a storm was coming.
For this reason, to save money.｜错在后面只接不定式短语，不是完整陈述句。应改为 For this reason, we decided to save money.
For this reason, so we left early.｜错在重复表达结果，for this reason 和 so 不要叠加。应改为 For this reason, we left early.

I was tired. For this reason because I slept late, I missed class.｜错在一个位置里同时塞入 for this reason 和 because 从句，结构混乱。应改为 I slept late. For this reason, I missed class. 或 Because I slept late, I missed class.

contrasts.title: for this reason vs. as a result
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都常放句首接完整句，但 for this reason 更强调“基于前述理由作出判断”，as a result 更强调“实际结果”。语气上前者更像论证，后者更像结果报告。对比：The evidence was weak. For this reason, we waited. / It rained all night. As a result, the game was canceled.

contrasts.title: for this reason vs. therefore
contrasts.note: 结构上 therefore 更灵活，可放句首、句中；for this reason 多放句首。语气上 therefore 更书面、更压缩；for this reason 更清楚，适合学习者写作。对比：The cost is high; therefore, we must cut spending. / The cost is high. For this reason, we must cut spending.

contrasts.title: for this reason vs. so
contrasts.note: 结构上 so 常直接连接口语中的因果句，for this reason 通常另起一句。语气上 so 很口语，for this reason 更正式。对比：I was tired, so I went home. / I was very tired. For this reason, I went home early.

contrasts.title: for this reason vs. that's why
contrasts.note: 结构上 that’s why 后面常接结果句或省略已知部分；for this reason 后面直接接完整陈述句。语气上 that’s why 更口语、更对话化。对比：I missed the bus. That’s why I was late. / I missed the bus. For this reason, I was late for class.

examples.sentence: The road was icy. For this reason, the school canceled classes.
examples.translation: 路面结冰了。因此，学校取消了课程。
examples.note: 最安全的基础用法：前一句给原因，后一句给结果。

examples.sentence: Many students lacked internet access. The plan was, for this reason, delayed by a week.
examples.translation: 很多学生缺少网络条件。这个计划因此被推迟了一周。
examples.note: 句中位置可以用，但比句首更书面，初学者不必优先模仿。

examples.sentence: The sample size was too small; for this reason, the findings should be treated with caution.
examples.translation: 样本量太小；因此，这些发现应谨慎看待。
examples.note: 学术或说明文里很自然。

examples.sentence: The train was delayed. As a result, we arrived late. For this reason, we should leave earlier next time.
examples.translation: 火车晚点了。结果我们到晚了。因此，下次我们应该更早出发。
examples.note: 先用 as a result 说事实结果，再用 for this reason 引出建议，能看出两者差别。

examples.sentence: My laptop keeps shutting down. For this reason, I need to back up my files today.
examples.translation: 我的笔记本电脑老是自动关机。因此，我今天得备份文件。
examples.note: 日常生活中也能用，但语气比 so 正式。

examples.sentence: The instructions were unclear. For this reason, several users made the same mistake.
examples.translation: 说明不清楚。因此，几个用户犯了同样的错误。
examples.note: 适合解释某个问题为什么发生。

templates.label: 安全初学者模板
templates.template: [原因句]. For this reason, [结论句].
slotHints: 先用完整句说明原因或事实。
后面写完整结果、判断或决定，不要只写短语。
templates.exampleSentence: The price is too high. For this reason, we will look for another supplier.
templates.exampleTranslation: 价格太高了。因此，我们将寻找另一家供应商。

templates.label: 句中书面模板
templates.template: [原因句]. [结论句], for this reason, [补充说明].
slotHints: 前半句先给出主要结论。
for this reason 后面补充进一步结果或限制，整体较书面。
templates.exampleSentence: The system is unstable. We should, for this reason, delay the launch.
templates.exampleTranslation: 系统不稳定。因此，我们应该推迟上线。

templates.label: 写作用模板
templates.template: [分析/事实]. For this reason, [建议/判断/结论].
slotHints: 前面适合放观察、数据、分析。
后面适合放 should, need to, must, it is important to 等结论表达。
templates.exampleSentence: The evidence is still limited. For this reason, more research is needed.
templates.exampleTranslation: 现有证据仍然有限。因此，还需要更多研究。

items.title: this shows that... / this means that...
items.pattern: this shows that... / this means that...
items.coreExplanation: this shows that... / this means that... 用来把前面的事实、数据、例子或结果“解释成一个结论”。that 后面必须接完整句子，不能只接名词。它常放在一句话后面单独起新句，也常放在分号、句号后，用来做分析或总结。
items.usageNote: 语气中性偏书面，最自然的场景是分析图表、解释结果、写作文、课堂回答或做逻辑说明。日常口语也能用，但连续多次使用会显得像报告。它不适合用来表达纯个人喜好；那时更自然的是 I think...。通常 this 指前一句或前面整件事，所以先给事实，再写 this shows/means that...。初学者最安全做法：前句写事实，后句写 This shows that + 完整结论。

slotSchema.label: 前文事实/证据
slotSchema.hint: 这里先放具体事实、数据、例子或结果。常见错误是前文太空泛，如 only 'It is good.'。更好：Sales increased by 20%.

slotSchema.label: 解释框架
slotSchema.hint: 固定用 this shows that 或 this means that。常见错误是漏掉 that 后面完整句子，或把 this 换成空指代。例：This shows that...

slotSchema.label: 结论句
slotSchema.hint: that 后面放完整判断句，要有主语和谓语。常见错误是只放名词短语，如 that the reason。正确：that the plan is working.

commonErrors: ✘ This shows the plan is useful.｜问题：少了 that，在初级写作里结构不稳。｜修改：This shows that the plan is useful.
✘ This means that a big problem.｜问题：that 后面不能只接名词短语。｜修改：This means that there is a big problem.
✘ People studied harder. This shows that.｜问题：that 后面缺少完整内容。｜修改：People studied harder. This shows that the new rule had an effect.
✘ This show that students need help.｜问题：主语是 this，动词要用 shows。｜修改：This shows that students need help.
✘ This means when prices rise.｜问题：means 后面应接 that 从句表达结论，不是残缺的时间从句。｜修改：This means that prices are rising.
✘ I like this movie. This shows that it is good.｜问题：把个人喜好硬说成客观证明，不自然。｜修改：I like this movie. I think it is good. / The high ratings show that it is popular.

contrasts.title: the fact is that...
contrasts.note: 结构上是 The fact is that + 事实，本身直接陈述“真实情况”；this shows that 是从前文证据推出结论。语气上前者更像澄清或强调真相，后者更像分析。对比：The fact is that we are late. / The traffic was heavy. This shows that we left too late.

contrasts.title: it seems that... / it appears that...
contrasts.note: 结构上都是 it + seems/appears + that，但它们表示“看起来”，不强调证据证明；this shows that 更像根据事实得出较明确结论。语气上 seems 更保留、更委婉。对比：It seems that he is tired. / He yawned several times. This shows that he is tired.

contrasts.title: I think (that)...
contrasts.note: 结构上 I think (that) + 观点，来源是说话者；this shows that + 结论，来源是前文证据。语气上 I think 更主观、口语化；this shows that 更客观、写作化。对比：I think the plan will work. / The test results are better. This shows that the plan will work.

contrasts.title: according to...
contrasts.note: according to + 来源/依据，如 according to the report，不直接下结论；this shows that 则把依据解释成结论。场景上 according to 常先标来源，this shows that 常后接分析。对比：According to the report, sales fell. / This shows that demand is weaker.

contrasts.title: this means that vs this shows that
contrasts.note: 两者都接 that 从句。this shows that 更像“这表明/显示”，强调证据指向；this means that 更像“这意味着”，强调结果或含义。写作里 shows 常配数据和现象，means 常配后果。对比：The score dropped. This shows that students struggled. / This means that extra support is needed.

examples.sentence: The number of errors fell by half. This shows that the new system is working.
examples.translation: 错误数量减少了一半。这表明新系统正在起作用。
examples.note: 最核心、安全的两句式：先给事实，再下结论。

examples.sentence: This means that we need more time, because two team members are still absent.
examples.translation: 这意味着我们需要更多时间，因为还有两名队员缺席。
examples.note: 也可把原因放在后面补充说明。

examples.sentence: The survey received only 35 responses; this shows that the sample is too small.
examples.translation: 这份调查只收到35份回复；这表明样本太小。
examples.note: 写作中常放在分号后，连接证据与分析。

examples.sentence: It seems that the class was difficult, but the low average score shows that many students could not follow it well.
examples.translation: 这门课似乎很难，但较低的平均分表明许多学生没能很好地跟上。
examples.note: 和 it seems that 对比：前者较委婉，后者证据更强。

examples.sentence: My phone battery drops very quickly. This means that I should replace it soon.
examples.translation: 我的手机电池掉电很快。这意味着我应该尽快更换它。
examples.note: 日常生活里也很自然，尤其用于“现象→含义/行动”。

examples.sentence: Many students now submit homework on time. This shows that the new rule has had a positive effect.
examples.translation: 现在很多学生都按时交作业。这表明新规定产生了积极效果。
examples.note: 适合考试作文中的分析句。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
templates.template: [事实]. This shows that [结论].
slotHints: 先写一个具体事实或结果，不要只写模糊评价
结论部分必须是完整句子，如 the method is effective
templates.exampleSentence: Sales increased in June. This shows that our campaign was successful.
templates.exampleTranslation: 六月的销售额上升了。这表明我们的宣传活动是成功的。

templates.label: 含义判断模板
templates.template: [现象]. This means that [后果/需要做的事].
slotHints: 前面放现象或变化
后面常写 need, should, will, there is/are 等完整判断
templates.exampleSentence: The road is closed. This means that we need to take another route.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这条路封了。这意味着我们需要走另一条路线。

templates.label: 句内连接模板
templates.template: [事实]; this shows that [结论].
slotHints: 前半句写证据，后面用分号接分析
后半句仍然要用完整 that 从句
templates.exampleSentence: The experiment failed twice; this shows that the method is unreliable.
templates.exampleTranslation: 实验失败了两次；这表明这个方法不可靠。

templates.label: 写作型加强模板
templates.template: [具体数据/result]. This shows that [较客观的分析结论].
slotHints: 前面尽量用数字、调查结果、观察结果
结论尽量写客观判断，少写纯个人喜好
templates.exampleSentence: Attendance fell from 90% to 60%. This shows that student interest has dropped.
templates.exampleTranslation: 出勤率从90%降到了60%。这表明学生兴趣下降了。

items.title: it is worth noting that...
items.pattern: it is worth noting that + clause
items.coreExplanation: it is worth noting that + 从句 用来提醒读者注意一个“值得特别看到”的信息，常见于正式写作、分析和说明中。that后面要接完整句子，不能只放名词。这个框架通常放在句首作引出，也可放在句中插入，后面常接逗号再继续说明。
items.usageNote: 语气偏正式、客观，常用于论文、报告、评论、演讲稿中强调一个补充但重要的点。日常聊天里一般太书面，口语中更自然的是 also, by the way, or the thing is。它不是简单“另外”，而是“这一点值得注意”。放句首最稳妥；若置于句中，前后要注意逗号。初学者先安全使用：It is worth noting that + 完整句子.

slotSchema.label: 固定框架
slotSchema.hint: 固定用 It is worth noting that。常见错误是漏掉 it 或写成 is worth to note。例：It is worth noting that sales increased.

slotSchema.label: that后的完整句子
slotSchema.hint: 这里要放完整陈述句，要有主语和谓语。常见错误是只放名词短语。例：that the policy affects small businesses.

slotSchema.label: 后续补充信息
slotSchema.hint: 常在主句后继续解释结果、原因或对比。常见错误是句子没收住，标点混乱。例：It is worth noting that prices fell, which surprised investors.

commonErrors: It is worth noting the result.｜错因：noting后这里少了that从句，原句只接了名词。｜改法：改成 It is worth noting that the result was unexpected. 或 The result is worth noting.
It is worth to note that prices fell.｜错因：固定搭配不是 worth to do。｜改法：用 It is worth noting that prices fell. 或 It is worth noting the fall in prices.
Worth noting that the plan failed.｜错因：正式完整句中通常不能省掉 it is。｜改法：写成 It is worth noting that the plan failed.
It is worth noting that the high cost.｜错因：that后不是完整句子，只有名词短语。｜改法：写成 It is worth noting that the cost is high.
It is worth noting, that the data is limited.｜错因：that前通常不断开；逗号位置不自然。｜改法：写成 It is worth noting that the data is limited. 或 It is worth noting, however, that the data is limited.
It is worth noting that, many students disagree.｜错因：that后不应立刻加逗号把从句切开。｜改法：写成 It is worth noting that many students disagree.

contrasts.title: the fact is that...
contrasts.note: 结构上都是接that从句，但 the fact is that 更像“事实是”，语气更直接甚至带纠正意味；it is worth noting that 是提示“这一点值得注意”，更像分析性提醒。比较：The fact is that we are late.（直接点明事实）/ It is worth noting that we are late.（把迟到当作一个需关注的点）

contrasts.title: in addition / moreover / besides
contrasts.note: 这些连接词后面直接接完整句子，用来“再加一点”；it is worth noting that 则先做评价框架，再引出内容，强调重要性更强、语气更正式。比较：Moreover, costs rose. / It is worth noting that costs rose.

contrasts.title: I think that...
contrasts.note: I think that 表主观看法，常见于口语和一般写作；it is worth noting that 更客观、更像作者在组织论证。比较：I think that this rule is unfair.（我认为）/ It is worth noting that this rule affects low-income families.（值得注意的是）

contrasts.title: it seems that...
contrasts.note: it seems that 表根据迹象作推测，不强调“提醒注意”；it is worth noting that 假设信息本身已值得被看见。比较：It seems that demand is falling.（看起来）/ It is worth noting that demand is falling.（这一点值得注意）

contrasts.title: according to...
contrasts.note: according to 后接来源，如人、报告、数据；it is worth noting that 后接完整内容。前者强调信息出处，后者强调作者要你注意。比较：According to the report, demand fell. / It is worth noting that demand fell.

examples.sentence: It is worth noting that this method is cheaper.
examples.translation: 值得注意的是，这种方法更便宜。
examples.note: 最基础、安全的用法：句首框架 + 完整句子。

examples.sentence: This method is, it is worth noting, cheaper than the old one.
examples.translation: 这种方法——值得注意的是——比旧方法更便宜。
examples.note: 句中插入可以出现，但不如句首常见；初学者少用。

examples.sentence: It is worth noting that the survey was conducted in rural areas only.
examples.translation: 值得注意的是，这项调查只在农村地区进行。
examples.note: 很适合学术或报告里提示研究限制。

examples.sentence: Moreover, sales increased in May. It is worth noting that profits did not.
examples.translation: 此外，五月销售额上升了。值得注意的是，利润并没有上升。
examples.note: 对比连接词：这里第二句不是单纯补充，而是提醒读者看关键反差。

examples.sentence: It is worth noting that not all students agreed with the decision.
examples.translation: 值得注意的是，并不是所有学生都同意这个决定。
examples.note: 常用来突出例外、限制或反面信息。

examples.sentence: It is worth noting that the store closes earlier on Sundays.
examples.translation: 值得注意的是，这家店周日关门更早。
examples.note: 虽然偏正式，但也可用于通知、说明等日常书面场景。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
templates.template: It is worth noting that + 完整句子.
slotHints: that后面一定写完整句子，不要只写名词
优先把整个框架放在句首，最自然
templates.exampleSentence: It is worth noting that the test is optional.
templates.exampleTranslation: 值得注意的是，这个测试是自愿参加的。

templates.label: 带补充说明模板
templates.template: It is worth noting that + 完整句子, + 补充结果/原因.
slotHints: 主句先说“值得注意”的内容
逗号后再补充结果、解释或影响
templates.exampleSentence: It is worth noting that attendance fell, which may affect the final result.
templates.exampleTranslation: 值得注意的是，出勤率下降了，这可能会影响最终结果。

templates.label: 写作用对比模板
templates.template: 连接词/前句. It is worth noting that + 对比或限制信息.
slotHints: 前一句先给背景，再用本结构强调关键限制或反差
很适合 however, moreover 等后面接第二句
templates.exampleSentence: The plan looks effective. It is worth noting that it is also expensive.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这个计划看起来有效。值得注意的是，它也很贵。
